programme.title: Commando - A One Man Army
programme.desc: Karan Dogra, an Indian commando, lands in China due to a plane crash. But Indian government term him as a terrorist.

programme.title: Sky Force
programme.desc: Amid escalating airstrikes between India and Pakistan, a group strives to uncover the truth behind the conflict, risking their lives to stop deadly consequences.

programme.title: Holiday-A Soldier Is Never Off Duty
programme.desc: Virat, a secret Defence Intelligence Agent on vacation in Mumbai, uncovers a bomb plot during a bus ride, leading him on a thrilling chase to catch the terrorists.

programme.title: Aitraaz
programme.desc: The man accused of rape sues for sexual harassment - who will the jury believe?

programme.title: Wanted
programme.desc: Bad boy gangster falls for good girl, but can he change his ways for love?

programme.title: Singh Is Kinng
programme.desc: Happy Singh, a simple villager, stumbles through a series of misadventures and unexpectedly becomes an underworld King, complicating his love life with Sonia, who despises criminals.

programme.title: The Dunes
programme.desc: A devoted domestic counselor fights to stop his ex-wife from moving the country with their son and her new partner, risking everything to prevent relocation altogether.

programme.title: Chakravyuh
programme.desc: Adil and Kabir infiltrate a Naxal group, but Kabir's loyalty shifts. Friendship turns deadly as ideals clash, forcing them into a battle neither imagined when they started.

programme.title: Game Paisa Ladki
programme.desc: Innocent girl's plight leads her to become a keeper for a psychotic don.

programme.title: Satyaprem Ki Katha
programme.desc: Satyaprem, a relatable middle-class guy, juggles his unrequited love for Katha as he supports her through a tough breakup, all while deepening their unexpected friendship.

programme.title: Lingaa
programme.desc: Lingaa, once a petty thief, reforms after learning his grandfather built a village dam. He dedicates himself to protecting the dam from a corrupt minister's destructive plans.

programme.title: Sirf Tum
programme.desc: Deepak finds a lost handbag and send it back on Aarthi's address. Thus they begin writing to eachother on regular basis and eventually fall in love. Will they able to meet eachother?

programme.title: K3 - Kaali Ka Karishma
programme.desc: A young man, living happily yet ghost-fearing, travels with his family to Coimbatore, Tamil Nadu, where a vengeful ghost possesses him, unleashing haunting dangers.

programme.title: Commando 3
programme.desc: A mysterious London-based foe plans an imminent attack. Karan Singh Dogra and British Intelligence team up to stop him. Will Commando save the country in time?

programme.title: Biwi No.1
programme.desc: Pooja, a simple housewife, lives with her husband and two children. However, when her husband leaves her for a beautiful model, Rupali, she gets a glamorous makeover to grab his attention.

programme.title: Baaghi
programme.desc: Ronny, expelled for mischief, enters a Kerala martial arts academy and forms a tense bond with Sia. Rivalry with powerful Raghav tests love, loyalty, and survival.

programme.title: Jim Button And The Wild 13
programme.desc: Jim and Luke embark on a perilous journey to safeguard Morrowland, hoping to uncover Jim's mysterious origins and uncover a truth that could change everything.

programme.title: Khamoshi
programme.desc: In a haunted mansion, a mute girl confronts sinister spirits and hidden truths, fighting against fear and darkness to uncover the chilling secret of her past.

programme.title: Table No. 21
programme.desc: Vivaan and Siya join game show and turns into survival challenge.

programme.title: Veere Di Wedding
programme.desc: Four friends seek love, but learn friendship has its own powerful impact.

programme.title: Valimai
programme.desc: Police officer Arjun relentlessly chases violent bikers after a deadly theft and murder, facing danger, tough choices, and time running out to stop them.

programme.title: Dream Girl
programme.desc: Sapna steals from the rich to maintain a home for orphans. Anupam takes her for a real princess and falls in love.

programme.title: Maine Pyar Kiya
programme.desc: Family conflicts test Prem and Suman's love. Will he win her heart?

programme.title: Mission Raniganj
programme.desc: Jaswant Singh Gill, a mining engineer, bravely leads a rescue mission to save 65 miners stuck inside a flooded coal mine, risking everything for their lives and safety.

programme.title: Bhajrangi Bhaijaan
programme.desc: A mute girl from a Pakistani village gets lost after India. In Kurukshetra, Pawan-Hanuman devotee-bonds with her while seeking parents, journeying toward Pakistan with reporter.

programme.title: Dabangg 3
programme.desc: Chulbul Pandey, now a formidable police superintendent, faces personal danger as old rival Bali Singh resurfaces, threatening his family amid escalating violence and confrontation.

programme.title: The Reunion
programme.desc: In 1997, three prep school friends hide a dark secret. In 2022, a gym wall is set to be torn down-ready to reveal the truth buried for years. Mystery is about to unfold.

programme.title: Aatma - Feel It Around You
programme.desc: After losing the custody of his daughter Nia to his wife, Maya, Abhay dies in a car accident. However, his spirit continues to haunt their life.

programme.title: Operation Romeo
programme.desc: Romantic evening turns terrifying when couple encounters mysterious strangers.

programme.title: Hotel Mumbai
programme.desc: A harrowing true story of courage during Mumbai's 2008 Taj Hotel siege, where staff, guests, and a protecting couple risk everything to save lives.

programme.title: Acharya
programme.desc: A stranger arrives in a corrupt village to fulfill a comrade's wish by ending illegal practices and challenging powerful leaders.

programme.title: Badhaai Do
programme.desc: Shardul, a gay guy, and Suman, a lesbian woman, enter into holy matrimony to appease their families. However, when Suman's girlfriend moves in with them, their lives become more complex.

programme.title: Dil Juunglee
programme.desc: A quirky romance unfolds between a rich English tutor and a small-town guy, as they navigate heartbreak, career dreams, and evolving feelings over the years.

programme.title: Satyaprem Ki Katha
programme.desc: Satyaprem, a relatable middle-class guy, juggles his unrequited love for Katha as he supports her through a tough breakup, all while deepening their unexpected friendship.

programme.title: Trapped
programme.desc: A man becomes trapped inside a bare, empty high-rise with no food, water, or power, forcing him to endure isolation, scarcity, and relentless uncertainty.

programme.title: Toofaan
programme.desc: The story of a boxer and the struggles he faces on his journey to get to the national level competition.

programme.title: Deva
programme.desc: Deva, a fearless rebel, rises against oppression with strength and conviction, leading a powerful journey of resistance, sacrifice and transformation.

programme.title: Aligarh
programme.desc: Dr. Siras, a quiet professor, becomes a media target over his sexuality. Through the turmoil, he finds unexpected comfort in the journalist covering his painful story.

programme.title: Welcome To New York
programme.desc: An unexpected trip to New York City sets a duo off on a comic adventure that changes their lives forever.

programme.title: Qarib Qarib Singlle
programme.desc: Two Contrasting Personalities Yogi And Jaya Meet Through A Dating App And Rediscover Themselves While They Set Out To Revisit Their Past.

programme.title: Citylights
programme.desc: Hoping to escape hardship, a farmer and his family leave their village behind and move to the city, dreaming of a better future amidst new challenges.

programme.title: Akaash Vani
programme.desc: Akaash and Vani are in love. But her parents force her to marry elsewhere because her sister eloped.

programme.title: Karthik Calling Karthik
programme.desc: A shy man starts receiving mysterious calls from himself, unraveling a gripping psychological twist tied to trauma, self-worth, and hidden fears.

programme.title: Vadh
programme.desc: After their son moves to USA, a middle-class married couple is relentlessly harassed by a loan shark.

programme.title: Bareilly Ki Barfi
programme.desc: In Bareilly, free-spirited Bitti embraces life on her own terms until she meets Chirag and Pritam, sparking unexpected changes in her journey.

programme.title: Manmarziyaan
programme.desc: Rumi and Vicky, who are in love, are pressurised to get married by Rumi's family. But Vicky refuses to commit and a broker brings in Robbie as a suitor.

programme.title: Omerta
programme.desc: Ahmed Omar Sheikh, driven by deep hatred, abducts and kills journalist Daniel Pearl, shaking the world with one of the most chilling acts of terrorism and revenge.

programme.title: Phone Bhoot
programme.desc: Inspired by stories from their childhood, two young men create a business around capturing ghosts. However, their endeavour turns dangerous when they must fulfil their promise to a poltergeist.

programme.title: The Tashkent Files
programme.desc: A journalist begins investigating the mysterious death of India's second Prime Minister. The deeper he digs, the more questions arise about truth, history, and suppressed political secrets.

programme.title: Sherdil - The Pilibhit Saga
programme.desc: In Pilibhit Tiger Reserve, people left elders for tigers seeking compensation.

programme.title: Saheb Biwi aur Gangster 3
programme.desc: Power, lust, and betrayal define the battle between royals and gangsters in this tale of political ambition and complex personal vendettas.

programme.title: Mere Pyare Prime Minister
programme.desc: A traumatised young boy, seeking justice for personal tragedy, writes to the Prime Minister and embarks on a brave journey across the country in search of truth and closure.

programme.title: Operation Romeo
programme.desc: A romantic evening spirals into trauma when a couple faces moral policing, triggering a dark and emotional journey of revenge and consequences.

programme.title: Sarkar 3
programme.desc: Gandhi seeks Sarkar's assistance for a project that will displace people. Sarkar refuses, triggering intense conflicts as power, politics, and betrayal take center stage in this gripping story.

programme.title: Veere Di Wedding
programme.desc: Kalindi's friends visit her after learning of her upcoming wedding. They travel to Thailand and embark on a journey of self-discovery.

programme.title: Sam Bahadur
programme.desc: Sam Manekshaw, a highly decorated Indian army officer, served for over four decades and fought in five wars before becoming the first army officer promoted to the rank of Field Marshal.

programme.title: Badla
programme.desc: Naina's lover is found dead, and she's accused. Hiring a top lawyer, she races against time to uncover the truth and save her freedom.

programme.title: Good Bye
programme.desc: A heartwarming story of the Bhalla family that depicts their journey of grief and self-discovery after they suffer the loss of their loved one.

programme.title: Uri: The Surgical Strike

programme.desc: College friends Ajay and Raj always play pranks on each other and then get into trouble. One day, Ajay needs his birth certificate for his passport, but he is shocked to find out his real name.

programme.title: Suriya S3
programme.desc: Uncompromising police chief Durai Singam battles against a criminal operation dealing in deadly toxic waste.

programme.title: Andaaz
programme.desc: Raj moves to Europe after his love Kajal marries another. When he decides to marry Jia, he discovers that she is Kajal's sister-in-law.

programme.title: Janta Ki Adalat
programme.desc: DSP Jyoti foils the plans of Shankar, a prisoner who threatens to kill a minister. But she lends her support to Shankar after she hears about the minister's dark secrets and ill intentions.

programme.title: Tele Shopping
programme.desc: Learn about a wide selection of products and take advantage of a convenient method of shopping. A show for those who love to shop from the comforts of their home.

programme.title: Jeene Do
programme.desc: Thakur Sher Bahadur Singh is a powerful landlord who gives Suraj a loan to pursue his studies in Mumbai. When Suraj leaves the village, the thakur exploits Suraj's father and his brother Karamveer.

programme.title: Tele Shopping
programme.desc: Learn about a wide selection of products and take advantage of a convenient method of shopping. A show for those who love to shop from the comforts of their home.

programme.title: Cirkus
programme.desc: Chaos reigns due to a case of mistaken identity when two sets of twins who were separated at birth coincidentally visit the same city.

programme.title: Vaishnavi
programme.desc: A girl born with divine powers to Venkata Narayana performs soothsayings annually, revealing hidden truths and guiding those in need.

programme.title: Dacait
programme.desc: A simple man transforms into a dacoit after being oppressed by the Zamindars of the area.

programme.title: Kishen Kanhaiya
programme.desc: Two brothers, one path of peace, the other of power, navigate family expectations, sacrifice, and love in this emotional drama of loyalty and redemption.

programme.title: Sansar
programme.desc: The story of a family which goes through tough times as relationships fail, with the leading lady finally succeeding in re-uniting everyone.

programme.title: Baazigar
programme.desc: A scheming killer is out to destroy a businessman's family after first killing his elder daughter.

programme.title: Keemat
programme.desc: Gopal makes Sudha go undercover to bust a racket selling young girls from India. But their assignment is compromised and death awaits them.

programme.title: Race
programme.desc: Saif Ali Khan, Bipasha Basu, Akshaye Khanna, Katrina Kaif, Anil Kapoor, Sameera Reddy

programme.title: Bombay Boys
programme.desc: Jackie Shroff,Deepa Sahi,Ritu Shivpuri

programme.title: Nazar Ke Samne
programme.desc: Jai, an unemployed drunk, gives false testimony that puts an innocent man in jail for murder. However, he later relents and tries to exonerate him by searching for the real killer.

programme.title: Sultanat
programme.desc: After his father is killed by General Khalid, Sultan tries to abduct Khalid's son. Khalid is after him unaware that Sultan is his own son.

programme.title: Badhaai Ho Badhaai
programme.desc: Anjali and Steven fall in love and elope. This brings an end to the friendship between the two families.

programme.title: Shiva Shakti
programme.desc: Shiva earns money for his sister Kusum's marriage. When Kusum gets pregnant with Shakti's child, Shiva vows to murder him.

programme.title: Phool Aur Kaante
programme.desc: Nageshwar entrusts his empire to son Ajay, but his discontented allies kidnap his grandson Gopal, threatening bloodline power and triggering a tense rescue.

programme.title: Ram Balram
programme.desc: Ram and Balram, live with their parents. Jagatpal, who kills the boy's parents, lies to the boys and promises to raise them himself.

programme.title: Market Raja Mbbs
programme.desc: A proud boss is now gentle because a student's spirit entered him. His friends await his change back. They want the old, tough leader they knew.

programme.title: Ranbanka
programme.desc: A non-violent engineer resorts to violence after his attempts to save his family from a local goon result in his son's murder.

programme.title: Keeping Up Appearances
programme.desc: Hyacinth has arranged one of her candlelight suppers, planning to impress Emmet, the director of the local amateur operatic society, with her singing. But circumstances beyond her control soon take over the proceedings.

programme.title: Sorry!
programme.desc: When Timothy's parents go on a second honeymoon, he plans to hold a party - with the intention of seducing Veronica Straddles.

programme.title: Snooker: World Championship
programme.desc: Coverage of day 2 of the World Snooker Championship at the Crucible Theatre, Sheffield.

programme.title: BBC Proms
programme.desc: The roof of the Royal Albert Hall nearly comes off in this special Prom dedicated to northern soul, curated by Wigan’s Stuart Maconie and Manchester’s Joe Duddell. A celebration of raw, rare American soul music that first obsessed young people across the north and the Midlands in the 1960s and 70s, inspiring an all-night dance culture. Epic new arrangements of northern soul anthems are performed by the BBC Concert Orchestra, conducted by a dancing Edwin Outwater, and featuring the vocals of six incredible singers led by Vula Malinga of Basement Jaxx fame. A night of freedom, passion and euphoria presented by Andi Oliver, with special guests Clarke Peters and PP Arnold.

programme.title: The Riviera: A History in Pictures
programme.desc: Two-part series in which Richard E Grant follows in the footsteps of artists who have lived, loved and painted on France's glorious Cote d'Azur. Revealing the intertwined relationship between modern art and the development of the French Riviera as an international tourist haven, Grant explores how impressionist painters Cezanne, Monet and Renoir first discovered the region in the 19th century, when the newly built railway arrived there. Captivated by the light and colour of this undiscovered landscape, the painters immortalised its shores on canvas, and in doing so advertised the savage beauty of the region. For neo-impressionists Paul Signac and Henri-Edmond Cross, the region provided a vision of utopia, while for Henri Matisse, the vivid colours of the area inspired him to adopt a new palette, and in doing so set modern art en route to abstraction. With visits to L'Estaque, St Tropez and Nice, Grant maps the progress of the region from cultural backwater to bohemian hotspot.

programme.title: Discovering the Music of Antiquity
programme.desc: When a music score is uncovered deep within the storerooms of the Louvre, musical historians scramble to realise the potential of this piece of papyrus. The text's grammatical features give us a clue to the composer's identity: Carcinus, an author cited by Aristotle in his Rhetoric. His name is engraved on a wall in the Parthenon, and the story of his life offers an insight into the history of Greek musicians, who were revered like gods and welcomed across the Mediterranean to take part in competitions modelled on the Olympic Games. The discovery of the papyrus, more specifically an ancient version of the tragedy entitled Medea, throws open a new mission by researchers to hear the music sung through modern arrangement. But to listen to the Medea as it was heard by the Greeks 2,400 years ago, it still has to be played on period instruments. From the Greek cities of Anatolia to the Ptolemies’ Egypt, from the mythical site of Delphi to the discoveries made in Pompeii, relive this voyage along the Mediterranean coast, where archaeological excavations have unearthed instrument remains.

programme.title: Genius of the Ancient World
programme.desc: Historian Bettany Hughes embarks on an expedition to India, Greece and China on the trail of three giants of ancient philosophy: Buddha, Socrates and Confucius. All three physically travelled great distances philosophising as they went and drawing conclusions from their journeys. With Bettany as our guide, she gets under the skin of these three great minds and shines a light on the overlooked significance of the 5th century BC in shaping modern thought across the world. In this first episode, Bettany investigates the revolutionary ideas of the Buddha.

programme.title: The Riviera: A History in Pictures
programme.desc: Two-part series in which Richard E Grant follows in the footsteps of artists who have lived, loved and painted on France's glorious Cote d'Azur. Revealing the intertwined relationship between modern art and the development of the French Riviera as an international tourist haven, Grant explores how impressionist painters Cezanne, Monet and Renoir first discovered the region in the 19th century, when the newly built railway arrived there. Captivated by the light and colour of this undiscovered landscape, the painters immortalised its shores on canvas, and in doing so advertised the savage beauty of the region. For neo-impressionists Paul Signac and Henri-Edmond Cross, the region provided a vision of utopia, while for Henri Matisse, the vivid colours of the area inspired him to adopt a new palette, and in doing so set modern art en route to abstraction. With visits to L'Estaque, St Tropez and Nice, Grant maps the progress of the region from cultural backwater to bohemian hotspot.

programme.title: Snooker: World Championship
programme.desc: Coverage of day 3 of the World Snooker Championship at the Crucible Theatre, Sheffield.

programme.title: Scotland: Rome's Final Frontier
programme.desc: Were the ancient Scottish tribes too much for the Roman Empire? Or was Scotland simply not worth conquering? Archaeologist Dr Fraser Hunter looks back on three centuries of contact and conflict with Scotland’s Roman invaders. The first Tay Bridge, the first depiction of tartan and forgotten Roman camps that once held thirty-five thousand men. A story of a superpower pitted against tribesmen and warlords, and one with fascinating modern parallels.

programme.title: Tutankhamun: The Truth Uncovered
programme.desc: What killed King Tutankhamun? Ever since his spectacular tomb was discovered, the boy king has been the most famous pharaoh of all ancient Egypt. But his mysterious death, at just 19 years old, has never been explained. Dallas Campbell reports on new scientific research being carried out on his fragile remains in an attempt to get to the truth. Using CT scan data, the programme creates the first scientifically accurate image of the king's corpse. DNA analysis uncovers a secret about Tutankhamun's family background, and the genetic trail of clues leads to a new theory to explain his death. This is an epic detective story that uncovers the extraordinary truth of the boy behind the golden mask.

programme.title: Art That Made Us
programme.desc: During the reign of Elizabeth I, Protestants and Catholics use art, language and new technology to wage a battle for power in the British Isles, creating surprising and often radical works.

programme.title: An Art Lovers' Guide
programme.desc: Janina Ramirez and Alastair Sooke discover the art and design of Amsterdam - a city that pioneered much of modern life, from multinational trade to how we design our homes.

programme.title: Tutankhamun: The Truth Uncovered
programme.desc: What killed King Tutankhamun? Ever since his spectacular tomb was discovered, the boy king has been the most famous pharaoh of all ancient Egypt. But his mysterious death, at just 19 years old, has never been explained. Dallas Campbell reports on new scientific research being carried out on his fragile remains in an attempt to get to the truth. Using CT scan data, the programme creates the first scientifically accurate image of the king's corpse. DNA analysis uncovers a secret about Tutankhamun's family background, and the genetic trail of clues leads to a new theory to explain his death. This is an epic detective story that uncovers the extraordinary truth of the boy behind the golden mask.

programme.title: Storyville
programme.desc: This two-part documentary is a personal journey by award-winning film-maker Ric Esther Bienstock that delves into the heart of the culture wars and freedom of speech conversations that have exploded on US campuses over the last ten years. Bienstock argues that the liberal ideals of open debate and diversity of thought are under siege. To begin with, campuses became front lines in a new moral conflict, where questions of race, gender and identity shaped not only what could be said, but who could speak, and at what cost. Then the pendulum swung. The backlash did not restore balance, it escalated the fight. Protests were curtailed, institutions seized, and free speech itself was turned into a political weapon.

programme.title: Britain's Nuclear Secrets: Inside Sellafield
programme.desc: Lying on the remote north west coast of England is one of the most secret places in the country - Sellafield, the most controversial nuclear facility in Britain. Now, Sellafield are letting nuclear physicist Professor Jim Al-Khalili and the television cameras in to discover the real story. Inside, Jim encounters some of the most dangerous substances on earth, reveals the nature of radiation and even attempts to split the atom. He sees inside a nuclear reactor, glimpses one of the rarest elements in the world - radioactive plutonium - and even subjects living tissue to deadly radiation. Ultimately, the film reveals Britain's attempts - past, present and future - to harness the almost limitless power of the atom.

programme.title: Horizon
programme.desc: Volcanoes have long helped shape the Earth. But what is less well known is that there are volcanoes on other planets and moons that are even more extraordinary than those on our own home planet. Horizon follows an international team of volcanologists in Iceland as they draw fascinating parallels between the volcanoes on Earth and those elsewhere in the solar system. Through the team's research, we discover that the largest volcano in the solar system - Olympus Mons on Mars - has been formed in a similar way to those of Iceland, how a small moon of Jupiter - Io - has the most violent eruptions anywhere, and that a moon of Saturn called Enceladus erupts icy geysers from a hidden ocean. Computer graphics combined with original Nasa material reveal the spectacular sights of these amazing volcanoes. Along the way, we learn that volcanoes are not just a destructive force but have been essential to the formation of atmospheres and even life. And through these volcanoes of the solar system, scientists have discovered far more about our own planet - what it was like when Earth first formed, and even what will happen to it in the future.

programme.title: BBC News
programme.desc: The latest news from the BBC.

programme.title: Arts in Motion
programme.desc: Arts in Motion reveals the creative lives and working habits of extraordinary artists. Cecilia Bartoli is one of the greatest living opera singers. From her roots in Rimini to universally esteemed opera houses, Cecilia traces her ascent to artistic excellence. Today, Cecilia leads her orchestra, her mission clear: to honour the genius of 18th-century composers and to preserve their legacy.

programme.title: BBC News
programme.desc: The latest news from the BBC.

programme.title: BBC News
programme.desc: Four astronauts. One historic mission. Join the four crew members embarking on Nasa's Artemis II mission, the first crewed journey to the moon in over fifty years. From training to take off, we see what it really takes to travel further from the earth than ever before. As the astronauts return to the moon, watch as they witness a total solar eclipse from deep space, navigate the unglamorous realities of survival in orbit, and ultimately return home with a dramatic splashdown in the Pacific Ocean.

programme.title: BBC News
programme.desc: The latest news from the BBC.

programme.title: AI Decoded
programme.desc: AI Decoded examines Sam Altman's vision for an era of artificial superintelligence. The Open AI CEO is calling for a new kind of social contract - an 'AI New Deal' - arguing that unprecedented technological power will require rethinking how the benefits are shared, including ideas such as wealth redistribution and even taxes on robots.

programme.title: BBC News
programme.desc: The latest news from the BBC.

programme.title: Global Eye
programme.desc: A weekly programme from the BBC World Service, bringing forensic journalism from all corners of the world by BBC Eye, the World Service's critically acclaimed investigative unit, and featuring expert global correspondents getting to the heart of the biggest international stories. This week, Farhat Javed looks at how Pakistan has positioned itself as a peace broker in the conflict between the US and Iran and BBC Eye investigates how routine visits for medical treatment in rural Pakistan have led to hundreds of children becoming infected with HIV, uncovering evidence of medical negligence and a failing public health system.

programme.title: BBC News
programme.desc: The latest news from the BBC.

programme.title: Rachel Khoo's Kitchen Notebook
programme.desc: Cook and food writer Rachel Khoo continues her culinary journey taking in the rural beauty of the historical province of southeastern France, Provence. Here, Rachel explores the mountain towns and olive groves and visits a friend where together they cook a delicious tarte tartin. Rachel also visits a goat farmer and cheesemaker and is inspired by the passionate food producers of Provence. Back home, Rachel makes baked goat's cheese cigars with a melon radish and cucumber slaw, provencal roast chicken with roasted fennel and haricot blanc salad, and a grape and raspberry brioche and butter pudding.

programme.title: The President's Path
programme.desc: Sumi Somaskanda, Bernd Debusmann and Courtney Subramanian discuss the ongoing war in Iran and look at what it means for Trump's legacy - balancing the 'win' of the assassination of the Ayatollah Ali Khamenei with the economic fallout of the war. They also look at the president's attack on the Pope and consider the impact that will have on his Catholic fan base.

programme.title: World Questions
programme.desc: Violent youth protests toppled the government and led to a general election in March. Nepal's Gen Z had demanded an end to corruption, less inequality and more jobs for the young. Now, the country is stepping into the unknown after the resounding electoral victory of a party pledged to their aims. The Rastriya Swatantra Party is only four years old and is being led into government by rapper turned politician Balendra Shah. They hold the hopes of many millions, but can they deliver? And do their supporters have the patience to wait? Jonny Dymond chairs a panel drawn from politicians and activists as they debate questions from a large audience in Kathmandu.

programme.title: Talking Business
programme.desc: How much of a threat are data centres facing? The physical infrastructure that AI depends on is growing at a rapid pace as companies pour billions of dollars into the technology that is increasingly important in our homes and jobs. But Iran has already targeted western firms' data centres in the Gulf amid its war with the United States. Ben Thompson explores their importance to the global economy and how well protected they are. Plus, he hears from the world's biggest producer of cork about the growing uses of this natural and sustainable material including in the Artemis II space mission.

programme.title: Match of the Day
programme.desc: Highlights of five Premier League matches, including Manchester United’s trip to Chelsea, with both clubs chasing a Champions League spot. It’s a big day in the relegation battle as Leeds take on Wolves and Spurs host Brighton. Elsewhere, Brentford face Fulham, and Newcastle welcome Bournemouth to St James’ Park. Presented by Gabby Logan with Joe Hart and Ashley Williams.

programme.title: Sunday with Laura Kuenssberg
programme.desc: Laura Kuenssberg talks to those making the news, inside and outside politics.

programme.title: Politics London
programme.desc: The latest political news, interviews and debate.

programme.title: Secrets of the Conclave
programme.desc: The papal conclave is a succession drama like no other. Triggered by the death of Pope Francis, 133 cardinals descend on the Vatican to elect his successor. But these cardinals are not just electors - they are candidates too. One of these 133 men will become pope. It is an ancient ritual of prayer and isolation that determines the future of the Catholic Church - and it all unfolds in secrecy. With unprecedented access to the Vatican, this documentary offers a rare insight into a ceremony that has decided the church’s leadership for centuries, featuring a glimpse of what happened inside that is told by those who were there.

programme.title: Homes Under the Hammer
programme.desc: A couple of challenging projects in Cambridgeshire and Lincolnshire mean the new owners will need to roll their sleeves up. In East Sussex, Jacqui sees a charming mid-terrace with beautiful sea views.

programme.title: Bargain Hunt
programme.desc: The teams are battling it out in Doune, Perthshire. Natasha Raskin Sharp meets leading tartan historian Peter MacDonald to learn about the origins of the iconic patterned cloth, while experts Roo Irvine and Danny Sebastian are guiding the reds and the blues.

programme.title: BBC Weekend News
programme.desc: The latest national and international news from the BBC.

programme.title: Weather for the Week Ahead
programme.desc: Detailed weather forecast.

programme.title: Songs of Praise
programme.desc: Aled Jones is at St Asaph Cathedral in north Wales for the Royal Maundy service, where HM the King honours Christians for outstanding service to church and community. Aled joins TM the King and Queen in the congregation at St Asaph and learns from the Lord High Almoner, the Right Reverend Graham Usher, Bishop of Norfolk, the history of the service and why it has been so special to the monarchy for over 800 years. Aled also meets two of the 154 Christians honoured by His Majesty, discovering how Dr Alan Mayer has made his church the centre of the village for 50 years and how Jan McTear serves a popular lunch to combat loneliness in her community. With glorious music to reflect the example of Jesus’ life of service. 1. The Church’s One Foundation – St Asaph Cathedral 2. Praise to the Holiest – Leeds Cathedral 3. How Great Thou Art – St Thomas' Church, Newcastle upon Tyne 4. The Lord’s My Shepherd – Crathie Kirk, Ballater 5. O Thou Who Gave Its Dawn Its Form (Tydi A Roddaist) – Aled Jones 6. Love Divine – St Asaph Cathedral

programme.title: Escape to the Country
programme.desc: Nicki Chapman is property shopping in Devon with a pair of entrepreneurial sisters who dream of creating their very own glamping business. Keen to discover more about the holiday industry, Nicki heads to Exmoor National Park, where she meets a couple who’ve transformed a Grade II listed manor house into a luxury bed and breakfast.

programme.title: Garden Rescue
programme.desc: Sisters Julia and Angela share a deep bond with Crete. They have visited the island since childhood, and even after they lost their parents, their annual pilgrimage remains a heartfelt tradition. Their garden is currently a mess, full of builders' clutter. It’s a space that needs transforming, and the sisters would love to infuse it with the essence of a Cretan holiday – blue and white hues, pergola, olive trees, lush greenery and sun-drenched corners. With a budget of £6,000, can designers Charlie and Chris bring the spirit of Crete to Yateley, transforming this space into a Mediterranean dream garden?

programme.title: Despicable Me
programme.desc: Delighting in all things wicked, supervillain Gru hatches a plot to steal the moon. Surrounded by an army of little yellow Minions, he has to incorporate three orphans into his evil plans.

programme.title: Secret Garden
programme.desc: David Attenborough reveals the hidden lives of foxes, hedgehogs, blue tits and frogs, each making a life for themselves in a small urban garden in Bristol.

programme.title: BBC Weekend News
programme.desc: The latest national and international news from the BBC.

programme.title: BBC London
programme.desc: The latest news, sport and weather from London.

programme.title: BBC Weather
programme.desc: The latest weather forecast.

programme.title: Countryfile
programme.desc: John Craven and Charlotte Smith are in the Peak District National Park, famed for its breathtaking landscapes and wildlife. But despite its wild and rugged appearance, there’s a huge amount of work needed to protect it. John spends the day as a ranger and meets volunteers who have devoted years to caring for this landscape. Charlotte jumps on a bike to explore an accessible cycling route and heads out across the moors with a scientist monitoring one of the park's most elusive residents – the mountain hare. Adam Henson is also in the Peaks to see what happens when farmers stop going it alone and start working together.

programme.title: Secret Garden
programme.desc: David Attenborough reveals the hidden struggles of swallows, field mice, newts and moles as they race to breed in a beautiful country garden in Cumbria.

programme.title: Antiques Roadshow
programme.desc: The roadshow marks the centenary of Queen Elizabeth II’s birth with a special episode filmed at Windsor Castle, featuring personal items and cherished mementoes.

programme.title: Queen Elizabeth II: Her Story, Our Century
programme.desc: Marking what would have been Queen Elizabeth II’s 100th year, this landmark documentary tells the story of her life alongside a century of momentous change in Britain. Through archive and eyewitness reflections, the film explores how she led the monarchy through profound social, political and cultural change. Contributions from some of Britain’s best-known faces and international figures, including Dame Helen Mirren, Sir David Attenborough, Barack Obama and Queen Camilla, reveal how she remained a constant in a changing world.

programme.title: BBC Weekend News
programme.desc: The latest national and international news from the BBC.

programme.title: BBC London
programme.desc: The latest news, sport and weather from London.

programme.title: Match of the Day
programme.desc: Arsenal travel to Manchester City in a game that could be crucial in deciding this season’s Premier League title race. Elsewhere, Everton face Liverpool in the first Merseyside derby at their new Hill Dickinson Stadium, Sunderland travel to Champions League-chasing Aston Villa, and Nottingham Forest continue their battle to beat the drop at Burnley. Mark Chapman presents alongside Wayne Rooney and Danny Murphy.

programme.title: The Claudia Winkleman Show
programme.desc: Claudia is joined by Dan Levy, creator, writer and star of the multi-award-winning show Schitt's Creek, who discusses his new venture, Netflix's Big Mistakes; actor Phil Dunster, who shot to fame in the hit show Ted Lasso, to talk about his new series, Rooster, on HBO Max; star of stage and screen Cush Jumbo, who chats about the return of the second season of her British crime thriller Criminal Record; and comedian Josh Widdicombe, who discusses his podcast The Museum of Pop Culture.

programme.title: Ambulance
programme.desc: Alistair attends to Paul, who has fallen onto concrete from a ladder. The specialist paramedic must assess his multiple injuries and reassure his patient, who fears he is dying.

programme.title: Weather for the Week Ahead
programme.desc: Detailed weather forecast.

programme.title: Joins BBC News
programme.desc: BBC One joins the BBC's rolling news channel for a night of news.

programme.title: Breakfast

programme.title: Morning Live
programme.desc: Join Gethin Jones, Helen Skelton, Michelle Ackerley and the team for conversation, advice and tips to make your day that little bit easier.

programme.title: Expert Witness
programme.desc: When eight-year-old Sarah Payne is murdered, a pollen expert finds a vital link between the suspect and the grave. In another case, a blood spatter expert is called when a body is found in a freezer.

programme.title: Homes Under the Hammer
programme.desc: We’re 'flat out', with three very different flats in London, Bath and Shropshire. Can the new owners who purchased them at auction bring them up to scratch?

programme.title: Bargain Hunt
programme.desc: Two teams of families go antiques-hunting at a huge fair in Kent, in the company of their experts Catherine Southon and JP, aka Jonathan Pratt.

programme.title: BBC News at One
programme.desc: The latest national and international news from the BBC.

programme.title: Shift the Thrift
programme.desc: In Bridgend, Anum and Betti race to turn charity shop finds into profit, risking big on designer items, bold upcycles and a high-stakes live-streamed auction.

programme.title: Designing the Hebrides
programme.desc: With Banjo’s design reputation growing, the Isle of Mull Rugby Club hire him to redesign their bar to reflect the club’s rich history. But with a large space to fill, and little knowledge of sport, Banjo is out of his creative comfort zone. With only a few days to complete the makeover, Banjo must face up to his past to find a way to complete this tricky brief. But how will his adventurous design go down with the club’s players and members?

programme.title: Escape to the Country
programme.desc: Ginny Buckley takes the helm as retirees Anthea and Keith look to upsize from their one-bedroom terraced cottage in a Hertfordshire town to a three-bedroom detached home in the Worcestershire countryside. Despite having viewed 16 properties, the couple have yet to find ‘the one’. Ginny presents them with several contenders, including a lovingly refurbished timber-framed Grade II listed cottage, a 125-year-old property with an exquisite country garden, and a Victorian home that maxes out on views. Exploring the magical Malvern Hills, Ginny also meets a family who quit the London rat race to establish an award-winning brewery.

programme.title: I Made It at Market
programme.desc: After coming to Dom’s business boot camp in the summer of 2023, stone carver Louise from Oxfordshire and stained-glass artist Charlotte from Bristol built dream businesses. Louise arrived at the boot camp, looking for help to make stone carving her full-time job. Now, Dom visits her studio to find out how she has done exactly that and how much money she makes from it. Charlotte reveals how she has built a sustainable stained-glass business after giving up her job in a vet’s practice and how much she now earns from her work.

programme.title: The Finish Line
programme.desc: Roman Kemp, with sidekick Sarah Greene, hosts the quiz show where contestants race in moving podiums across the studio floor. Correct answers get the players moving, while wrong answers will stop them in their tracks. Five contestants – Simon, Charlotte, Jessica, Conor and Lizzy – battle across a series of fast-paced qualifiers and head-to-head races where the aim of the game is simple – make it to the finish line before your rivals. The winner then races against the track itself in an attempt to win the £5,000 cash prize.

programme.title: Pointless
programme.desc: Alexander Armstrong is joined by co-host Tom Allen for the brainy quiz where the contestants try to score as few points as possible by plumbing the depths of their general knowledge to come up with the answers no-one else could think of.

programme.title: BBC News at Six
programme.desc: The latest national and international news from the BBC.

programme.title: BBC London
programme.desc: The latest news, sport and weather from London.

programme.title: Party Election Broadcasts: Green Party
programme.desc: A party election broadcast by the Green Party for the local and mayoral elections on 7 May.

programme.title: The One Show
programme.desc: Alex Jones and Roman Kemp bring you the stories that matter from across the country.

programme.title: EastEnders
programme.desc: Priya tries to help Ravi, Nugget stands up for himself, and Lauren explores a new business venture.

programme.title: Panorama
programme.desc: Reports of antisemitic incidents have risen in the UK over the last few years, leaving many British Jews feeling fearful and under threat. In October 2025, a knifeman tried to enter Heaton Park synagogue - two members of the congregation were killed. Judith Moritz examines how abuse, intimidation and violence aimed at Jewish communities has become more widespread, hearing from the government’s independent reviewer of terror legislation who believes the normalisation of antisemitism makes violence more likely.

programme.title: What Happened at...?
programme.desc: The 1986 explosion at Chernobyl triggered a public health emergency across Europe. Today, the site's strategic location continues to be a flashpoint in Russia's war with Ukraine. Forty years on from the disaster, Jordan Dunbar travels to Chernobyl to examine the events that led to the world's worst nuclear accident and what we can learn from them today.

programme.title: Mint
programme.desc: Shannon is obsessed with the love story of how her parents met - all she wants is her own romance to sweep her off her feet. But finding love is tough when you’re the only daughter of a notorious crime family. When Shannon meets Arran, she thinks her luck has finally changed. She feels the spark. But will her family allow it when they realise who he is?

programme.title: Mint
programme.desc: Dylan announces a shocking decision about the business. It leaves Cat and Ollie stunned and sends the family’s future into doubt. But Shannon is only thinking of Arran. Can they have something special despite the world around them? Arran, meanwhile, is rebuilding a relationship with his ambitious brother Liangelo. It becomes apparent that Arran’s split loyalties might pose a problem for Shannon.

programme.title: BBC News at Ten
programme.desc: The latest national and international news, with reports from BBC correspondents worldwide.

programme.title: BBC London
programme.desc: The latest news, sport and weather from London.

programme.title: Have I Got News for You
programme.desc: Gabby Logan hosts, with guest panellists comedians Miles Jupp and Michelle Wolf joining team captains Paul Merton and Ian Hislop as they delve into the news.

programme.title: Thirteen Lives
programme.desc: A rescue mission is assembled in Thailand where a group of young boys and their football coach are trapped in a system of underground caves that are flooding. Based on a true story.

programme.title: Weather for the Week Ahead
programme.desc: Detailed weather forecast.

programme.title: Joins BBC News
programme.desc: BBC One joins the BBC's rolling news channel for a night of news.

programme.title: Breakfast
programme.desc: The latest news, sport, business and weather from the BBC's Breakfast team.

programme.title: Morning Live
programme.desc: Join Gethin Jones, Helen Skelton, Michelle Ackerley and the team for conversation, advice and tips to make your day that little bit easier.

programme.title: Expert Witness
programme.desc: When a body is found in a wheelie bin in a lake, a cell site analyst tracks the suspects’ mobile phones. And when a man disappears, a soil expert helps identify his killers.

programme.title: Homes Under the Hammer
programme.desc: Martel meets an old friend in County Durham who appears determined not to learn from his previous mistakes. Martin is pleased with the area he’s in, just maybe not the property. Tommy thinks a demolition derby might be in order in Kent.

programme.title: Bargain Hunt
programme.desc: Eric Knowles presents from Kingston in Surrey, with the reds and the blues led by experts Thomas Forrester and Gary Pe. Both teams are eager to make a profit at the auction, but who will win? While the competition hots up, Eric gets on his bike at the Herne Hill Velodrome.

programme.title: BBC News at One
programme.desc: The latest national and international news from the BBC.

programme.title: Shift the Thrift
programme.desc: In Midsomer Norton, couple Polly and Callum hunt for high-end labels and vintage glass to raise funds for Macmillan. Polly finds a steal in designer footwear, Callum gambles on a dressing table.

programme.title: Designing the Hebrides
programme.desc: To launch a new spirit distilled on the cheese farm where he lives, Banjo is asked by farm owner Chris to design a rustic gin tasting bar using salvaged materials found on the farm. This design is a deeply personal journey for Banjo, as he aims to give back to the people who welcomed him and his husband Ro into their community. But with a launch party looming, and an ambitious plan to complete, will the design live up to expectations?

programme.title: Escape to the Country
programme.desc: A couple from a busy seaside resort dream of a new life in Somerset for their young family, with outside space for chickens, llamas and maybe even a pig. Denise Nurse takes on their countryside search, lining up a lovely selection of homes, including one with spectacular views across the Bristol Channel to Wales. She also meets a creative pair who have already made their escape to the country and gets hands on with some screen printing.

programme.title: Make It at Market
programme.desc: Metalworker Diane from Northumberland and signwriter Sam from London are clocking into Dom’s bootcamp, hoping to build life-changing businesses. Diane, a mum of two, was an international businesswoman when she discovered a love for metal sculpting. Now it’s all or nothing as she’s determined to turn that passion into a full-time job, so she can be around more for her two girls. Mentor Ian Gill thinks she needs to improve the finish of her pieces to succeed and live out the dream of swapping a business suit for a welding jacket. After being diagnosed with a rare spinal condition, the stakes couldn’t be higher for plumbing and heating engineer Sam, who needs to find a new, less physical job. A self-confessed signwriting obsessive, he’s determined to turn it into a new career, but mentor Joby Carter thinks he’s got a job on to improve the quality of his work. Will Sam be able to find the time to knuckle down and make the improvements needed to build the business that would transform his life?

programme.title: The Finish Line
programme.desc: Roman Kemp, with sidekick Sarah Greene, hosts the quiz show where contestants race in moving podiums across the studio floor. Correct answers get the players moving, while wrong answers will stop them in their tracks. Five contestants – Maria, Paul, Said, Max and Albert – battle across a series of fast-paced qualifiers and head-to-head races where the aim of the game is simple – make it to the finish line before your rivals. The winner then races against the track itself in an attempt to win the £5,000 cash prize.

programme.title: Pointless
programme.desc: Alexander Armstrong is joined by co-host Angela Scanlon for the brainy quiz where the contestants try to score as few points as possible by plumbing the depths of their general knowledge to come up with the answers no-one else could think of.

programme.desc: The latest national and international news from the BBC.

programme.title: The One Show
programme.desc: Alex Jones and Roman Kemp bring you the stories that matter from across the country.

programme.title: EastEnders
programme.desc: Ravi is determined to keep his family together, Mark tries to help Lauren, and Elaine and Ian attend a gala.

programme.title: Interior Design Masters with Alan Carr
programme.desc: Alan Carr kicks off the competition by tasking ten interior designers to show off their signature styles by transforming a beach hut on the Dorset coast. They must also demonstrate their ability to reuse and repurpose where they can. Michelle Ogundehin and guest judge Jonathan Adler must decide who has created the Stand Out Space and which designer will be the first to go home.

programme.title: MasterChef
programme.desc: It’s the first heat of this year’s competition, and six talented cooks are braced for culinary battle. Their first goal is to secure one of four gleaming MasterChef aprons.

programme.title: BBC News at Ten
programme.desc: The latest national and international news, with reports from BBC correspondents worldwide.

programme.title: BBC London
programme.desc: The latest news, sport and weather from London.

programme.title: Four Weddings and a Funeral
programme.desc: Over the course of five social occasions, a committed bachelor must consider the notion that he may have discovered love.

programme.title: The Repair Shop
programme.desc: The team revive a powered football wheelchair, some meticulously handcrafted jewellery, a bagatelle board, and a speedway flag that tells a remarkable Glaswegian love story.

programme.title: Two Pints of Lager and a Packet of Crisps
programme.desc: Gaz decides it's time to make a proper commitment to Donna. Janet tries talking dirty for a living, and Louise is in for a shock when she finally meets her real parents.

programme.title: Two Pints of Lager and a Packet of Crisps
programme.desc: Sitcom about the lives and loves of five twentysomethings in Runcorn. Donna gives Gaz the answer to his marriage proposal. Janet finds a new way of providing for her child - the only problem is, it's illegal. Louise meets her real dad for the first time, but he is more interested in getting to know Jonny.

programme.title: BBC Comedy Short Films
programme.desc: Four very different schoolgirls are thrown together in detention. As they plan their escape, the situation spirals out of control.

programme.title: Doctor Who: Unleashed
programme.desc: Steffan Powell catches up with David Tennant and Catherine Tate as they film Doctor Who: The Star Beast. David gives us a tour of the Tardis, and we meet Yasmin Finney, who plays Rose, for the first time. We also welcome the original comic book creators Pat Mills and Dave Gibbons on set, where they meet the Meep for the first time. Stay tuned for a sneak peek of the second sixtieth anniversary special, Wild Blue Yonder.

programme.title: Doctor Who (2005–2022)
programme.desc: The Doctors embark on their greatest adventure in this 50th anniversary special. In 2013, something terrible is awakening in London's National Gallery; in 1562, a murderous plot is afoot in Elizabethan England; and somewhere in space an ancient battle reaches its devastating conclusion. All of reality is at stake as the Doctor's own dangerous past comes back to haunt him.

programme.desc: It’s the day of Vicki and Ross’s stag dos, Jasmine starts to feel part of the Slaters, and Bea makes a decision about her love life.

programme.title: EastEnders
programme.desc: A situation escalates for Vicki, Oscar attempts to avoid Josh, and Bea grows jealous of Honey.

programme.title: EastEnders
programme.desc: Vicki starts to become paranoid, Priya continues to worry about Ravi, and Oscar puts Lauren and Max to the test.

programme.title: EastEnders
programme.desc: Vicki is out of options, and Max and Lauren continue to compete.

programme.title: Sort Your Life Out
programme.desc: Stacey and the team head to Yorkshire to help the Mann-Monro family, a blended household of six that has been overwhelmed by years of clutter and emotional memories.

programme.title: Yesterday
programme.desc: Following a road accident, struggling singer-songwriter Jack Malik wakes up in an alternative reality where no-one seems to have ever heard of The Beatles. As he plays their songs and almost unwittingly starts to take credit for them, his career suddenly takes off.

programme.title: BBC Comedy Short Films
programme.desc: Animated sketch comedy set on a seemingly quiet residential street where the neighbours are all just trying to get through the day without incident. But beneath the calm exterior, everyday life quickly unravels into the absurd, the awkward and the unexpectedly hilarious - from dodging a secret crush to desperately trying to impress the builder. Whether it’s a piano teacher adapting to a four-legged student, a cycling enthusiast battling to stay inside his own outfit, or a work-from-home mum constantly thrown off course, life on the street is anything but peaceful. Blending sharp observational humour with bursts of slapstick chaos, the show transforms suburban routine into a playground of comic mayhem - where absolutely anything can happen.

programme.title: Behind Bars: Sex, Bribes and Murder
programme.desc: An investigation into a viral prison sex tape leads journalist Mobeen Azhar to discover an illicit prison economy fuelled by inappropriate relationships, coercion and contraband.

programme.title: Behind Bars: Sex, Bribes and Murder
programme.desc: Mobeen’s investigation into prison corruption hears of the murder of a former officer who had been involved in uncovering an illicit relationship between a guard and an inmate.

programme.title: Ashley Cain: Into the Danger Zone
programme.desc: Brazil’s favelas are considered extremely dangerous, making them one of the world’s most deadly places to grow up. As a result of poverty and a lack of government and police control, many young men here resort to drug dealing and gang crime under the control of the drug cartels. In other neighbourhoods, heavily armed criminal militia groups control day-to-day life with an iron grip, extorting residents – fail to pay or cross them, and the consequences can be deadly. Ashley meets young men living in some of Rio’s most notorious favelas to find out what it’s like to live here and what forces them to join gangs to survive. He will learn what it takes to become a lookout, foot soldier and drug dealer in a world where death could become a reality at any moment. And he’ll come face to face with a gun-toting militia boss to uncover how these men rule the favelas through fear and intimidation.

programme.title: Ashley Cain: Into the Danger Zone
programme.desc: South Africa has for years battled the scourge of rhino poaching, fuelled by insatiable demand for their horns in Asia. Ashley embarks on a mission to find out who is behind these brutal acts, discovering how the challenges young men face growing up in deprived rural communities can lead to illegal poaching. Hanging out with men from KwaZulu-Natal – or Zululand – he earns their trust and joins them on a legal hunt for bushmeat in order to understand what day-to-day life is like here. He immerses himself in all sides of the issue - meeting an illegal poacher who is horrifyingly responsible for over 150 rhino deaths and a family whose son was killed while attempting to hunt a rhino. He even joins antipoaching units to see the measures being used to protect these incredible creatures.

programme.title: Deadly 60
programme.desc: In this Deadly 60 special, wildlife expert Steve Backshall examines the increasing pressures threatening our planet's animal populations, including tigers, gorillas and whales. He explores the various conservation efforts set up to help them and reveals a few success stories, such as the remarkable comeback of Africa's white rhino.

programme.title: Deadly 60
programme.desc: Steve Backshall comes face to face with the world’s deadliest animals and investigates the deadly threats to their survival.

programme.title: Sort Your Life Out
programme.desc: Emotions run high as Stacey Solomon and her team help single mum Steph and her three daughters transform their home through a life-changing declutter and budget-friendly makeover.

programme.title: Stacey Dooley
programme.desc: The Crown Prosecution Service has decided to take the women’s rape cases to trial. But with court delays and barrister strikes, will they ever get their day in court?

programme.title: Stacey Dooley
programme.desc: Facing court delays and the anxiety of preparing for court, Stacey follows the young women, who question if they can continue. Will they get the outcome they are hoping for?

programme.title: Stacey Dooley
programme.desc: Stacey tackles the subject of death by immersing herself in the world of undertaking at a century-old funeral director's.

programme.title: Ashley Cain: Into the Danger Zone
programme.desc: Marseille has been France’s drug capital for over 50 years, but the violence of the city's drug war is escalating as rival gangs kidnap, torture, kill and even set one another on fire. Ashley spends time in the notorious northern suburbs known as the 'cités', meeting young men involved in fighting the bloody drug war. He also rides along with the police on a night-time patrol. To understand the present, Ashley needs to learn of Marseille’s past, so he meets a former gangster who was part of the city’s infamous French Connection drugs trade in the 1970s and 80s. Ashley also meets people trying to steer young men away from gang life, video calls a gang boss behind bars and meets a small-time dealer who became a deadly hitman.

programme.title: Sort Your Life Out
programme.desc: Emotions run high as Stacey Solomon and her team help single mum Steph and her three daughters transform their home through a life-changing declutter and budget-friendly makeover.
