programme.title: Deadly 60
programme.desc: Steve travels to Gabon for the first time. Exploring Loango National Park, Steve goes in search of crocodiles, surprisingly deadly chimpanzees and feisty forest elephants.

programme.title: Deadly 60
programme.desc: Steve sets sail to Bali for a diving adventure to find a giant ocean sunfish. He rescues fish trapped in an abandoned net and meets the teenagers fighting to ban plastic bags on the island.

programme.title: Race Across the World
programme.desc: The five teams bid farewell to Europe and head into Asia, racing toward the remote riverside town of Halfeti, where the last to arrive will be eliminated from the competition.

programme.title: Better Date Than Never
programme.desc: Olivia can’t wait to head out on her first ever date, and Charles is excited to meet his match, but Nirvali struggles with nerves as she prepares for a blind date.

programme.title: Better Date Than Never
programme.desc: Dianne is ready to find love as her authentic self, Charles gets guidance from a relationship expert, and Nirvali braves her first date as Olivia moves on to her second.

programme.title: Stacey Dooley
programme.desc: Invited into the traditionally private Gypsy community, Stacey discovers the complex balancing act young women face growing up in one of Britain’s most maligned ethnic minorities.

programme.title: Stacey Dooley
programme.desc: Spending time with people who regularly steal from shops, Stacey gets to grips with the shoplifting epidemic and what it’s like for the frontline staff trying to prevent thefts.

programme.title: The Royal Hotel
programme.desc: Broke backpackers Hanna and Liv, working in a remote mining community’s run-down pub, are seen as fresh meat by the mainly male punters, and the boundaries of acceptable behaviour soon begin to blur.

programme.title: The Capture
programme.desc: With Pierson deemed MIA, Carey finally gains full control of SO15 and immediately releases Whitlock, leaving Figgis with no choice but to act. The Correction Inquiry hears a shocking revelation from Sir Rowan Gill, thrusting Garland into the glare of the world’s media. Pierson takes the initiative and, with some help from Wizard, gets back on his feet. E-Squadron go on the offensive, and Carey becomes entangled in an increasingly complex and dangerous situation. Every action she takes appears to have been predicted and premeditated. With few allies left to trust, sacrifice is inevitable – just how far is Rachel Carey willing to go to protect the truth?

programme.title: Babies
programme.desc: Lisa tries to find a way of getting Stephen to talk to her. Amanda splits up with Dave before beginning the move to her new life.

programme.title: This is BBC Two
programme.desc: Highlights of programmes on BBC Two.

programme.title: Take a Hike
programme.desc: This week, we’ve been tramping around the vast Highlands and Islands of Scotland, famous for its monster lochs. Loch Ness alone holds more water than all the lakes in England and Wales combined. We may well glimpse Nessie this week, but we’ll definitely see these five keen walkers who want to bag the big prize. Today, it’s the turn of 26-year-old Katrina, the tattoo shop manager from Glasgow. She’s been hiding an aquatic secret from the others. She has a part-time job as a mermaid, performing in aquariums. Katrina's walk starts and finishes on the shores of Loch Long - a haven for dolphins and porpoises, with some of the clearest water in Britain. Will she manage to persuade the walkers in for a dip? And can she pull out all the stops to take the lead?

programme.title: Countryfile
programme.desc: John Craven and Charlotte Smith head to the National Forest in the Midlands as it reaches a milestone moment: the planting of its 10-millionth tree. They discover how three decades of regeneration have transformed formerly scarred mining land into a thriving mosaic of woodland and rich wildlife habitat. John and Charlotte meet the people whose lives have been reshaped by the forest, from a women’s walking group discovering the countryside on their doorstep to the craftspeople building sustainable livelihoods from woodland materials. In Devon, Adam Henson meets a young farming couple reviving the practice of traditional woodland grazing.

programme.title: Breakfast
programme.desc: The latest news, sport, business and weather from the BBC's Breakfast team.

programme.title: Landward
programme.desc: Landward explores one of the biggest threats facing Scotland’s fragile rural communities - depopulation. Over the next decade, the population of 14 Scottish rural council areas is expected to decline. The team visits the places battling the issue and meets the people fighting to reverse the trend. In north west Scotland, Anne visits the secondary school on the sharp edge of depopulation, with just 31 pupils. Rosie discovers how hard it is to find housing in popular tourist hotspots, and Dougie travels to Arran to hear about the scheme convincing doctors to settle on the island. Meanwhile, Arlene meets the campaigners who think they have some solutions.

programme.title: Beechgrove Garden
programme.desc: Calum Clunie kicks off his season at Beechgrove, demonstrating what can be grown from a container in just one season. He also starts a season of productive gardening, showing what can be produced for a family of four from a medium-sized vegetable garden. Meanwhile, as summer approaches, Brian is looking at how to keep lawns looking great and tackling a patio space around Beechgrove’s chiminea corner. There is a visit to a great new allotment in Perth, and there are all the usual handy hints.

programme.title: Snooker: World Championship
programme.desc: Coverage of day 2 of the World Snooker Championship at the Crucible Theatre, Sheffield.

programme.title: Andi Oliver’s Fabulous Feasts
programme.desc: Andi Oliver heads to Cornwall to help local chef Ben Quinn put on an epic feast to thank NHS staff at the Royal Cornwall Hospital. Her idea of holding a feast for 80 people at one long table on a beach is a logistical nightmare - but it promises to be spectacular. Andi sets about devising a unique menu that tells the story of modern and traditional Cornwall using the best local produce. But will the weather hold, will she find the perfect live music to set the scene, and can she create a truly unforgettable thank you feast?

programme.title: The Best Dishes Ever
programme.desc: Ainsley Harriott introduces a selection of classic recipes from some of the BBC's best-loved television chefs. In this episode, Ainsley picks dishes influenced by the exciting cuisine of some of the world's most exotic places. The menu includes tapas from James Martin, Paul Hollywood's tortilla tower, a Jamaican ginger cake from the Hairy Bikers and Ainsley's own delicious jerk pork dish.

programme.title: Snooker: World Championship
programme.desc: Coverage of day 2 of the World Snooker Championship at the Crucible Theatre, Sheffield.

programme.title: Flog It!
programme.desc: This episode contains a collection of interesting and previously unseen finds from the show's travels around the country, with visits to Margam Country Park in south Wales, Highcliffe Castle in Dorset, Sandon Hall in Staffordshire and Powderham Castle in Devon. The antiques experts include Catherine Southon, Adam Partridge, Philip Serrell, Mark Stacey, Claire Rawle and David Fletcher, with items ranging from some folk art Scandinavian nutcrackers to a Napoleonic prisoner of war handcrafted box. Paul also takes a look around the stately Firle Place in East Sussex.

programme.title: Artemis: To the Moon and Back
programme.desc: The inside story of the Artemis II mission - filmed over three and a half years at NASA. How did four astronauts travel to the moon, going deeper into space than ever before?

programme.title: This Farming Life
programme.desc: King Charles visits Campbeltown Farmers' Market, where Andrew and Gayle hope to impress with their premium plants. Emma and Rob are holding a family wedding on the farm, but their pregnant cows could go into labour at any moment. And Callum and Zara hold a historic sheepdog trial, attracting competitors from afar - but Callum’s sheep don't go easy on him.

programme.title: Forensics: The Real CSI
programme.desc: Firearms officers from West Midlands Police receive a tip-off about a local man with an unexpected arsenal of firearms. Inside a bedroom wardrobe are six guns, two of which they believe are 3D-printed assault weapons. Along with the guns, the police also find ammunition, a silencer and a 3D printer. The 30-year-old resident is arrested and taken into custody. He has no criminal record and no obvious links to organised crime, leaving police teams to ask whether he is an extremist, an arms dealer or simply a gun collector, as he claims to be. Is he creating these guns to use them, and could he have used them already?

programme.title: The King's Speech
programme.desc: Following the scandalous abdication of his brother King Edward VIII, Bertie, who has suffered from a debilitating speech impediment all his life, is suddenly crowned King George VI of the United Kingdom. With his country on the brink of war and in desperate need of a leader, his wife Elizabeth seeks out the help of eccentric speech therapist Lionel Logue. Based on the true story of King George VI, The King's Speech is the inspirational tale of one man's quest to find his voice.

programme.title: Snooker: World Championship
programme.desc: Highlights from the second day of the World Snooker Championship in Sheffield.

programme.title: Question Time
programme.desc: Fiona Bruce presents an hour of debate from Cardiff, the first of Question Time’s special election programmes in Wales, Scotland and England. On the panel: for Labour, Wales’s deputy first minister Huw Irranca-Davies; Rhun ap Iorwerth, the leader of Plaid Cymru; Dan Thomas, the leader of Reform UK Wales; Darren Millar, the leader of the Welsh Conservatives; Jane Dodds, the leader of the Welsh Liberal Democrats; and Anthony Slaughter, the leader of the Wales Green Party. Watch or listen first on the BBC News Channel, iPlayer and Sounds at 9pm.

programme.title: Babies
programme.desc: Lisa is pregnant, and following her journey to the birth, we see 18 years into the future as her son prepares to leave for university. Dave tries to connect with his son.

programme.title: Sort Your Life Out
programme.desc: Stacey and the team head to Yorkshire to help the Mann-Monro family, a blended household of six that has been overwhelmed by years of clutter and emotional memories.

programme.title: This is BBC Two
programme.desc: Highlights of programmes on BBC Two.

programme.title: Bargain Hunt
programme.desc: Most people capture famous landmarks on their cameras, but Roo Irvine meets a man who makes them out of matchsticks! Michael Tolley measures the dimensions of Carlisle’s buildings by stepping them out. Then, he recreates them in miniature, using hundreds of matchsticks. So far, he has made 87 models, including a replica of an entire street. Over the border in Kelso, can the teams strike any good deals when they go bargain-hunting with experts David Harper and Irita Marriott? The blues are twin sisters Wendy and Gayle, although they weren’t born on the same day! Motorcycling fan Wendy is quick to spot a vintage oil can, while Gayle takes a shine to a white metal pin cushion. Over with the reds, friends Sue and (another) Wendy hope to get a pair of Victorian grape scissors for a snip. At Paul Laidlaw’s auction, the bids come in thick and fast, and at the end, one bonus buy sets hearts pounding.

programme.title: Secret Garden
programme.desc: David Attenborough reveals the hidden lives of foxes, hedgehogs, blue tits and frogs, each making a life for themselves in a small urban garden in Bristol.

programme.title: Match of the Day
programme.desc: Arsenal travel to Manchester City in a game that could be crucial in deciding this season’s Premier League title race. Elsewhere, Everton face Liverpool in the first Merseyside derby at their new Hill Dickinson Stadium, Sunderland travel to Champions League-chasing Aston Villa, and Nottingham Forest continue their battle to beat the drop at Burnley. Mark Chapman presents alongside Wayne Rooney and Danny Murphy.

programme.title: BBC News
programme.desc: Twenty-four hours a day, the latest national and international stories as they break.

programme.title: Snooker: World Championship
programme.desc: Coverage of day 3 of the World Snooker Championship at the Crucible Theatre, Sheffield.

programme.title: Talking Snooker
programme.desc: Snooker superfan Alistair McGowan takes a trip through the BBC archives for an affectionate look at the lives and careers of three of snooker's best-loved and most charismatic stars - Alex 'Hurricane' Higgins, Jimmy 'Whirlwind' White and Ronnie 'the Rocket' O'Sullivan. We look at the early television appearances that captured each of them before they really hit the big time, chart the ups and downs of their careers and examine programmes ranging from Wogan to Top Gear to get a real sense of why each of this trio were, in their day, known as 'The People's Champions'.

programme.title: Politics Live
programme.desc: The big political issues of the day.

programme.title: Snooker: World Championship
programme.desc: Coverage of day 3 of the World Snooker Championship at the Crucible Theatre, Sheffield.

programme.title: Richard Osman's House of Games
programme.desc: Every day this week, celebrity contestants Sam Campbell, Cherry Healey, Anthony Ogogo and Catherine Tyldesley take on a series of quiz rounds selected by the host, quiz legend Richard Osman. A daily winner is declared following a quickfire round at the end of each show, and the scores are tallied across the week, resulting in an overall champion being crowned on Friday. Will the winning stars opt for the much-coveted House of Games decanter, or will they be tempted by the House of Games retro bag?

programme.title: Great Japanese Railway Journeys
programme.desc: Michael begins a journey in and around Kyushu, the southernmost of Japan’s four main islands, and southern Honshu. He begins his exploration in Kagoshima, an extraordinary city which sits in the shadow of Japan’s most active volcano, Sakurajima. In the Sakurajima International Volcano Centre, Michael discovers how scientists use the latest technology to predict and warn of future volcano eruptions. He rides one of Japan’s quirky themed express trains to Ibusuki, a coastal town where he goes sand bathing. Inland is Chiran, the site of a former World War II airbase and now the Chiran Peace Museum. Michael learns of 439 young pilots who flew from Chiran to their deaths in the notorious kamikaze missions of the Pacific War.

programme.title: Global Eye
programme.desc: Bringing forensic journalism from all corners of the world.

programme.title: Mastermind
programme.desc: Clive Myrie presents the grand final of the classic quiz where six contenders return to the black chair in a bid to become the Mastermind champion and take home the coveted Glass Bowl. The specialist subjects are Dame Julie Andrews, the films of Danny Boyle, Beatrix Potter, Robert Burns, Notre-Dame de Paris and the astronaut Jim Lovell.

programme.title: University Challenge
programme.desc: It’s the grand final. Who will lift the series trophy? Amol Rajan asks the questions.

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: It’s finals week, where the winners from the heats compete to cook a course at a red-carpet banquet in Liverpool’s St George’s Hall to celebrate the British film industry. The eight rivals from all corners of the UK compete over a different course, from starter through to dessert, where they will be marked out of ten by an expert judging panel, including a surprise guest judge - in this episode, film director Marli Siu. It’s a nerve-wracking first day as the chefs settle into the kitchen and meet their rivals. Each wants to win a course at the banquet and get their name on the menu. Can they improve on their scores from the first rounds?

programme.title: Disclosure
programme.desc: Reporter Myles Bonnar travels to Ghana to look into Charmain's death. There, he interrogates the police investigation that was carried out at the time, and meets those who were at the scene, as he searches for the truth about what happened in room 112.

programme.title: Newsnight
programme.desc: Insights and global interviews on the news stories of the day. With Victoria Derbyshire.

programme.title: Michael Jackson: An American Tragedy
programme.desc: In August 1991, Michael signs the most lucrative deal in music history with Sony Music and prepares for what his team believes will be a landmark year. Then, a 13-year-old boy accuses Michael of child sexual abuse. Detectives describe the shock of investigating the world’s most famous performer, while inside Michael’s camp, aides recall disbelief and a frantic effort to keep Michael’s worldwide tour, business empire and public image intact. As the story breaks, police search Michael’s home, and the media storm intensifies. Michael publicly denies the allegations, condemning what he calls a 'trial by media'.

programme.title: Snooker: World Championship
programme.desc: Highlights of the snooker action on day three of the World Championship in Sheffield.

programme.title: Countryfile
programme.desc: John Craven and Charlotte Smith head to the National Forest in the Midlands as it reaches a milestone moment: the planting of its 10-millionth tree. They discover how three decades of regeneration have transformed formerly scarred mining land into a thriving mosaic of woodland and rich wildlife habitat. John and Charlotte meet the people whose lives have been reshaped by the forest, from a women’s walking group discovering the countryside on their doorstep to the craftspeople building sustainable livelihoods from woodland materials. In Devon, Adam Henson meets a young farming couple reviving the practice of traditional woodland grazing.

programme.title: Forensics: The Real CSI
programme.desc: It’s the early hours of the morning when a frantic teenage girl calls 999 to say that her friend has been attacked and stabbed in a car park. Police rush to the scene, where they find a 16-year-old boy with a life-threatening stab wound to his stomach. While his life hangs in the balance, the case is escalated to West Midlands Police’s homicide team, and forensic investigators get to work. They discover two discarded weapons at the scene - a kitchen knife and a machete.

programme.title: Race Across the World
programme.desc: The five teams bid farewell to Europe and head into Asia, racing toward the remote riverside town of Halfeti, where the last to arrive will be eliminated from the competition.

programme.title: This is BBC Two
programme.desc: Highlights of programmes on BBC Two.

programme.title: I Made It at Market
programme.desc: After giving them business-building action plans at his life-changing makers’ boot camp, Dom catches up with textile weaver Shiv from Brighton and portrait artist Jill from Berkshire to find out how they have turned them into the long-term, money-making careers they dreamt of. After seeing her at his boot camp in the summer of 2024, Dom drops in on Shiv at her studio to find out how her business has taken off and exactly what that means for her bottom line. In Berkshire, Jill reveals how her action plan led to her giving up her nursing job and how much she now earns from her art.

programme.title: Bargain Hunt
programme.desc: Two teams of families go antiques-hunting at a huge fair in Kent, in the company of their experts Catherine Southon and JP, aka Jonathan Pratt.

programme.title: Farm 999
programme.desc: Across the north of England, police close in on an organised crime gang targeting farms and stealing hi-tech GPS systems worth hundreds of thousands of pounds. After months of painstaking detective work, coordination across multiple forces and help from farmers on the ground, a high-stakes sting operation is launched to bring the thieves to justice. In Leicestershire, a huge barn fire devastates a family-run dairy farm, threatening cattle and destroying vital winter feed. As the fire ignites, owner Deborah watches helplessly from her hospital bed, and husband Blaise and daughter Millie race to save their livestock. Firefighters battle through the night to bring the blaze under control. And in Scotland, reporter Paul Colgan meets farmer Debbie Duffus as she is reunited with the air ambulance team who saved her life after a brutal farm accident.

programme.title: Lost and Found in the Lakes
programme.desc: The team assemble to take on a search for a heartbroken teen, who watched his phone, full of memories of his grandparents, sink to the bottom of a quarry. A local community project attempts to make a bike pulled from the swamp rideable again. And military dog tags found on the beach – but are they what they seem?

programme.title: BBC News
programme.desc: Twenty-four hours a day, the latest national and international stories as they break.

programme.title: Snooker: World Championship
programme.desc: Coverage of day 4 of the World Snooker Championship at the Crucible Theatre, Sheffield.

programme.title: Politics Live
programme.desc: The big political issues of the day.

programme.title: Snooker: World Championship
programme.desc: Coverage of day 4 of the World Snooker Championship at the Crucible Theatre, Sheffield.

programme.title: Richard Osman's House of Games
programme.desc: Every day this week, celebrity contestants Sam Campbell, Cherry Healey, Anthony Ogogo and Catherine Tyldesley take on a series of quiz rounds selected by the host, quiz legend Richard Osman. A daily winner is declared following a quickfire round at the end of each show, and the scores are tallied across the week, resulting in an overall champion being crowned on Friday. Will the winning stars opt for the much-coveted House of Games decanter, or will they be tempted by the House of Games retro bag?

programme.title: Great Japanese Railway Journeys
programme.desc: Off the west coast of Kyushu lies the Amakusa archipelago, formed of around 12 islands. Michael first takes a bullet train to the coast and then a ferry to the islands, where he is greeted by a group of island women performing the colourful, traditional haiya dance originally sung to fishermen. Michael also travels to Sakitsu, a tranquil town where Portuguese missionaries brought Christianity to Japan in the 16th century. After the religion was banned during the Edo period, worshippers hid from the authorities and became known as 'hidden Christians'. Returning to the mainland of Kyushu, Michael takes a regional train up to Kumamoto, where he encounters one of Japan’s most beloved mascots and dives into the story of one of the country's most revered historical figures, Saigo Takamori. Finally, in Shin Omuta, Michael meets Mr Matsunaga, a master swordsmith whose craft hasn’t changed for centuries.

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: Actor Will Poulter, who studied chef skills for his role in The Bear, joins Tom Kerridge, Lorna McNee and Phil Wang on the judging panel. They’re tasked with picking a winner from eight fish courses, which are all inspired by the British film industry. With Tom remarking that every single piece of fish is cooked perfectly, the judges have their work cut out in picking a winner, and the decision goes right to the wire. Only one of the chefs will earn the right to cook their fish course at the banquet.

programme.title: Britain's Biggest Warship
programme.desc: With just a few weeks to go before HMS Queen Elizabeth arrives in New York in spectacular style, the F35 jets try a method of landing never attempted before.

programme.title: QI XL
programme.desc: Sandi Toksvig and Alan Davies are joined by Neil Delamere, Cariad Lloyd and Chris McCausland for a vulgar episode of QI.

programme.title: To Think Like a Killer
programme.desc: Ann delves deeper into the dark minds of serial killers while balancing her life as a mother of four. Meanwhile, the Behaviour Science Unit races to prove profiling works.

programme.title: Mortimer & Whitehouse: Gone Fishing
programme.desc: In a first for Paul and Bob, they venture to southern Ireland to fish the mighty Lough Corrib. At 43.5km long, it is Ireland’s largest lough and one of the best places in the country to try trout fishing. This is very different to fishing the Hampshire chalk streams, as they did a few years ago - this time they are out on boats and try out a new technique called dapping. Good local knowledge of the weather and currents are essential, and they have local guide Richie Johnston to assist. Patience as well as good fishing know-how are always essential for any guide when working with Paul and Bob, especially when the three of them are sharing a boat. Later on, they head to a traditional Irish pub to meet old friend and Undertones singer Fergal Sharkey.

programme.title: Newsnight
programme.desc: Insights and global interviews on the news stories of the day. With Victoria Derbyshire.

programme.title: To Think Like a Killer
programme.desc: Ann’s landmark study reshapes criminal investigation. Serial crime falls, but the battle is only just beginning.

programme.title: Extraordinary Portraits
programme.desc: Jordan and Cian watched their mum lose her life at a young age to a rare form of dementia. Years later came another devastating blow as they found out they also carry the faulty gene, meaning they will almost certainly develop symptoms in their forties. Refusing to let the diagnosis define them, the brothers are determined to make their time count. Together, they began running for charity to raise awareness and funds for dementia research, turning fear and grief into purpose and action. Bill Bailey picks Unza Saleem to create a lasting legacy for the brothers, but as she is getting to grips with the portrait, she has her own family emergency which threatens to derail the project entirely.

programme.title: Snooker: World Championship
programme.desc: Highlights from the fourth day of the World Snooker Championship in Sheffield.

programme.title: Aadmi 1993
programme.desc: Vijay, a factory supervisor, is falsely accused and arrested for killing his family. Just when he is at his lowest point, an inspector offers to help him in return for capturing the son of a gangster.

programme.title: Aa Gale Lag Jaa 1994
programme.desc: Roshni, a wealthy heiress, wants to marry Suraj, a poor man, but her mother disapproves as his father is an ex-prisoner. Meanwhile, a series of murders take place and the suspicion falls on Suraj.

programme.title: Aapas Ki Baat
programme.desc: Kajal and Anand are two lovers who are separated when Kajal's family refuses to accept their relationship. Years later, fate brings them face-to-face with each other.

programme.title: Naalayak
programme.desc: Laxman has always been the black sheep of the family. He swindles his brother’s money to impress a girl.

programme.title: Aadmi Aur Insaan
programme.desc: Jai and Munish's friendship is ruined when both of them fall in love with the same girl, Meena. Things take a turn when Munish finds out Jai was involved in the case of a faulty railway bridge.

programme.title: Zamana
programme.desc: Ravi and Vinod's father is killed by a gangster for developing a photo that depicts a crime scene. Ravi, a criminal, and Vinod, a police officer, decide to exact revenge on their father's murderer.

programme.desc: Anju, a beautiful and spoilt woman, falls in love with a mysterious man, Shekhar. However, with time she realises that he has other motives.

programme.title: Khote Sikkay
programme.desc: An unknown horseback rider is after Jhanga, an evil and troublesome gangster who has terrorised everybody in the village, because he is responsible for his father's death.

programme.title: Dhund
programme.desc: After crashing his car on a foggy night, a man seeks help at a nearby home, where he encounters a dead man and a woman holding a gun. After hearing her sad story, he helps her hide the crime.

programme.title: Ustaad
programme.desc: Shamsher Singh, a runaway criminal, pretends to love Asha and gets married to her. However, on the wedding night, Shamsher leaves Asha and never returns.

programme.desc: The life of a couple gets completely changes when they inquire the whereabouts of the girl's lover who is found after been missing for three years.

programme.desc: A girl's father selects a groom for her who happens to be the son of his employer but on the day of engagement, she not only rejects but also humiliates him.

programme.title: Dushman Duniya Ka
programme.desc: Mahesh, an honest man, is shocked to learn that his son, Lucky, has been using recreational drugs. Lucky promises to kick the habit but circumstances push him towards the world of drugs once again.

programme.title: The Murderer: Hamilton Palace
programme.desc: Rocky, a well-known singer and performer at an elite hotel, becomes the prime suspect when Maheema, a news reporter who was deeply in love with him, is found dead.

programme.title: Karobaar: The Business of Love
programme.desc: Friends Rajiv and Amar go their separate ways after Seema decides to be with Amar rather than Rajiv. Years later, prosecution lawyer Amar is delighted to see Rajiv in court and accused of murder.

programme.title: Train to Pakistan
programme.desc: The people of Mano Majra, a village on the Indo-Pak border, live in harmony. However, things change when a train arrives from Pakistan containing the dead bodies of the people coming to India.

programme.title: Zakhm
programme.desc: A boy, born into a family of varying religions, finds himself embroiled in communal riots. Soon, his mother's death becomes an issue raised to fulfil political agendas.

programme.desc: Premi makes a living by selling poems to Benni and falls in love with a courtesan, Laila Jaan. However, he leaves her after finding the truth about her but tries to get back with her.

programme.desc: The first-ever registered encounter by the Mumbai Police, which took place on November 1, 1982. Based on a true story.

programme.desc: Mumbai of 1980s is terrorised by a serial killer who ruthlessly kills pavement dwellers in their sleep. A suspended police officer then decides to step in and conduct an investigation.

programme.desc: Three man staying under same roof are in search of true love. They meet their ladies but will these man be able to convince them to be their soul mates?

programme.desc: A waiter, is in love with the famous TV reporter Greeta Kapoor. After a man is murdered, Kapoor shows up at Raju's door to ask him some questions - it turns out that Raju served the dead man his last supper.

programme.title: Kitne Door... Kitne Paas
programme.desc: Jatin and Karishma, who meet each other on a plane to India, are set to marry the partners chosen by their respective parents. However, they find themselves attracted to each other.

programme.title: Tada
programme.desc: An honest man turns to crime to avenge his father's death and ends up being a don in Mumbai. He has to fight with enemies, former allies and a corrupt police officer.

programme.title: Teri Payal Mere Geet
programme.desc: Premi makes a living by selling poems to Benni and falls in love with a courtesan, Laila Jaan. However, he leaves her after finding the truth about her but tries to get back with her.

programme.title: Sarkar
programme.desc: The Indian version of The Godfather, with the focus on SARKAR, an Indian politician.

programme.title: Once Upon A Time In Mumbai Dobaara
programme.desc: The story of Shoaib, an underworld don who came to power by killing his mentor and has extended his kingdom with the help of his best friend, Javed, and his former lover, Mumtaz.

programme.title: Main Tera Hero
programme.desc: A notorious boy decides to attend college in Bangalore, but instead falls in love with a girl who has to marry a terrorizing police officer.

programme.title: Lootera
programme.desc: Lootera set in the year is 1953, is a love story of Pakhi (Sonakshi Sinha) and Varun (Ranveer Singh). She lives with her father, a Zamindar, in an aristocratic world. She falls in love with a visiting archaeologist, but he holds a secret...

programme.title: Kyaa Super Kool Hain Hum
programme.desc: Adi and Sid want to make big but always run out of luck and series of confusion start taking place when they reach Goa.

programme.title: This is CBBC!
programme.desc: Tune every day from 7.00am.

programme.title: Shaun the Sheep
programme.desc: Shaun steps in a can of red paint, and his resulting red hoof makes him a style icon on the farm. Before long, the rest of the flock try their own new looks and soon steal the limelight. As the outfits become more and more outrageous, Shaun wonders if less might sometimes be more.

programme.title: Danger Mouse
programme.desc: Animated series. Danger Mouse has to face his only fear when Penfold accidentally awakens a gigantic World Wide Spider.

programme.title: Dennis & Gnasher Unleashed!
programme.desc: Dennis recruits Grizzly Griller to help make school sports day more extreme, but as he and JJ get competitive against each other, who is sabotaging both their chances of winning?

programme.title: Boy Girl Dog Cat Mouse Cheese
programme.desc: While riding their scooters at the park, the bickering kids meet the Smiths. A brother and sister who don't seem to argue like our gang does - in fact, the two Smiths are super-supportive of each other! When they start pushing the other kids around, Boy, Girl, Dog, Cat, Mouse and Cheese challenge the Smiths to a race around the park! But our heroes have one problem - Cat doesn't know how to ride a scooter! The kids take turns teaching her and, when race day comes, Cat will really need to kick it into high gear if she wants to help the family beat those smug Smiths!

programme.title: Newsround
programme.desc: Topical news magazine for children.

programme.title: SpongeBob SquarePants
programme.desc: SpongeBob and Patrick exchange tiny living versions of themselves.

programme.title: Grizzy and the Lemmings
programme.desc: The Lemmings get a magical Mayan disc that transforms them into mutants.

programme.title: Sketchy Comedy
programme.desc: A plane, a chair, a pencil and a watering can are among the items getting the Sketchy treatment!

programme.title: Duck and Frog
programme.desc: Duck and Frog have one simple job: press buttons in a lift - and they can’t even do that right. Can they get the mayor to the ground floor in time for a grand opening?

programme.title: Duck and Frog
programme.desc: When looking after their neighbours' baby, Duck soon learns that he is a terrible babysitter - though it seems kids love Frog! Duck needs Frog to help out for once but soon loses the baby.

programme.title: Counterfeit Cat
programme.desc: Max tries to get out of bath night by cloning himself.

programme.title: Bro's in Control
programme.desc: Callum is a Little Bro in control! His Big Bro Adam is going up against best mate Joe to see who will win a series of funny, gungy and gross challenges, and who’ll take on a forfeit from the Wheel of Doom. Callum isn’t taking any nonsense, so they have to work hard to impress him! The National Art Gallery has called: they need replicas making ASAP! It’s a job for Big Bro and Big Joe... using flour and gunge, natch. But whose art will be fit for a gallery, and whose will fail? Little Bro Callum decides the winner in this epic art showdown. Not all is lost, as the loser gets a chance to redeem themselves in the No Hands Challenge. Who can set up a Date Night using only their pockets, their wits and a giant shark with a violin? Sound extra? Callum’s challenges definitely are in this hilarious, gross and gungy contest!

programme.title: Gladiators: Epic Pranks
programme.desc: Every single Gladiator gets their revenge on Nitro. He thinks he is filming a science show, but phoney interruptions and bogus camera hiccups lead to an epic pie pandemonium finale!

programme.title: Operation Ouch!
programme.desc: The doctors investigate how fast farts travel with their Fart-O-Matic contraption. And Gladiator Dynamite tests Dr Dan and Dr Raph’s bulging biceps in an epic Body Battle!

programme.title: Girl Troop vs Aliens
programme.desc: Pippa, Kiara and Ruby race to call for help, Tilda is trapped aboard the spaceship, and Ginny faces a tough choice, all while Brighthawk Camp waits out the alien threat.

programme.title: Danny and Mick
programme.desc: With Joy having been let down by the builders, Danny and Mick step up and offer to build the leisure centre's new sauna themselves. But can they follow flat-pack instructions? And will their antics get Joy hot and bothered?

programme.title: Danny and Mick

programme.title: DIY Deadly with Steve Backshall
programme.desc: In this unprecedented time of lockdown there has never been a better time to learn about the wildlife around us, and Deadly 60 fans have been sending in their videos, photos and questions for Steve Backshall to answer. In this episode, Steve focuses on our parks and gardens. This green space may look pristine, but for wildlife it’s savannah, jungle and mountains all rolled in to one. And judging by CBBC’s most observant viewers, its packed full of deadly animals! Steve unravels the mystery of some blue eggs in a nest, explains how a spider uses camouflage and trickery to lure in its prey, and tells about a worm that isn’t a worm... it’s a lizard! In a DIY demo, Steve then shows us how to keep hedgehogs warm and toasty during winter by building a hibernation house. But that’s not all. The audience gets their chance to ‘beat Backshall’ in a 60-second quickfire round. The question is, how many can Steve get right before his time is up? We also learn that fox cubs are endearing alarm clocks, and why you wouldn’t want to wander around an Australian garden without a grown-up!

programme.title: Art Ninja

All this with a helping of ninja art - a fake plaster cast, a 3D initial and some lovely prank soap.

programme.desc: In this unprecedented time of lockdown there has never been a better time to learn about the wildlife around us, and Deadly 60 fans have been sending in their videos, photos and questions for Steve Backshall to answer. In this episode Steve explores our aerial assassins, and judging by CBBC’s most observant viewers, the skies above us are home to a huge variety of deadly animals! Steve unravels the mysteries of a bird nest and a feather collection, explains that kites are spreading far and wide, and how to bring birds to your bird table without making them dinner themselves! In a DIY demo, Steve then shows us how to make fat ball dessert for our feathered friends, before basking in butterfly, bee and wasp glory! But that’s not all. The audience gets their chance to Beat Backshall in a 60 second quickfire round. The question is, how many can Steve get right before his time is up? In a fun-filled, fast and furious 13 minutes, we’ll also get to see some of the best bits from 13 years of deadly exploration. In this episode Steve gets hunted down by a silent forest predator, and catches the world’s biggest and most feisty wasp!

programme.title: TikTok: Murder Gone Viral
programme.desc: Documentary series exploring extraordinary true crime cases linked to the world's fastest-growing social media platform. When the bloodied body of a young girl was found by two dog walkers in the leafy woodland of a Cheshire park in February 2023, two teenagers were seen running away across a field - paramedics could not save 16-year-old Brianna Ghey, who had been stabbed 28 times. Her death set TikTok alight, with millions mourning the trans girl who had gathered over 60,000 followers and was becoming a social media star - with unique access for the first time to Brianna's father Peter Spooner, this episode follows the investigation which led to the convictions of 15-year-olds Scarlett Jenkinson and Eddie Ratcliffe. Brianna's murder triggered a huge outpouring of shock and grief on social media, and especially so amongst the young trans community.

Contestants are confronted with 22 sealed boxes with each one hiding a different amount of money - without knowing what each one contains, the contestant must eliminate the boxes, which are opened one at a time, and the amount of money inside is revealed. Pressure mounts as the player is tempted by the notorious Banker to accept an offer of cash in exchange for what might be inside their box, and as long as the larger cash prizes remain unchosen, the Banker's offers will get higher... but that can all change in an instant if a big cash prize is unveiled.

programme.title: Lewis
programme.desc: An empty Oxford college is the venue for an August bank holiday weekend attended by professional quiz contestants - when Lewis and Hathaway are called in after outstanding competitor Ethan Croft is found floating dead in the college fountain, they learn that Croft was an outrageous flirt with a number of secrets and the pair race to uncover the killer before the gathering breaks up. Meanwhile, Hathaway discovers that Lewis was planning a romantic weekend at Glyndebourne with a mystery date...

programme.title: Lewis
programme.desc: When a university friend of Hobson is found staked through the heart on Halloween night, Lewis and Hathaway are approached during their investigation by a medium who claims that they are in grave danger - the following day sees a student murdered in the house that Hobson shared with her university friends while a clue at the crime scene leads Lewis and Hathaway to a third victim. As the body count rises, Lewis and Hathaway are forced to acknowledge that Hobson might know more about the deaths than she is letting on...

programme.title: Lewis
programme.desc: Oxford's last surviving all-female college is bidding farewell to one of its most prominent professors - during a reunion of her brightest students, one of the group is found murdered at the bottom of a flight of stairs. Lewis is convinced that the killing is connected to a brutal attack which occurred at the college ten years before - as the investigation progresses, he and Hathaway are drawn ever deeper into the mystery of the college and its secrets.

programme.title: Doc Martin
programme.desc: Comedy-drama series about a GP who relocates to the sleepy Cornish hamlet of Portwenn. Martin and Pauline are surprised by the number of patients coming to see the Doc on the last day before his surgery closes and he moves to London - after Edith comes to see Martin to discuss the events of the previous night, taxi driver Tommy delivers a patient to the surgery but complains of having migraines himself. Elsewhere, Bert has cooked up a new scheme to recycle used vegetable oil from his restaurant, Mrs Tishell makes a unique leaving gift for the Doc, and Louisa's teaching assistant Tasha causes concern for Martin as she appears to be a little dizzy - when Tasha then collapses at Portwenn's Harbour Day celebrations, the Doc is shocked to learn that she is married to Tommy and that he is driving Louisa to her hospital appointment. Fearing that Louisa's life in danger, Martin rushes after her but instead finds her safe at the scene of a road accident - she then goes into premature labour...

programme.title: Doc Martin
programme.desc: Comedy-drama series. Martin is struggling to come to terms with fatherhood as the baby boy born to him and his estranged partner Louisa is about to change their lives dramatically - the curmudgeonly GP with no bedside manner had planned to resume his high-flying career as a consultant in London and moved out of the surgery to make way for new GP Dr Di Dibbs, but the arrival of the baby means that the Doc has to adjust his plans as he moves into Louisa's house to help with the baby and what is an awkward situation for both of them. Meanwhile, Dr Dibbs is newly-qualified and excited at the prospect of moving into her first surgery with husband Gavin as the practice manager, but Martin begins to have serious doubts about the competency of the new GP after she misdiagnoses a patient, dishes out prescriptions for totally inappropriate drugs, and even misdiagnoses her own illness with almost fatal consequences - elsewhere, Martin is unprepared for a devastating personal blow involving Joan.

programme.title: Rosemary & Thyme
programme.desc: Light-hearted drama series about a pair of gardening enthusiasts-turned-amateur sleuths. Laura and Rosemary are asked to restore The Dell, an ornate water garden cascade at the luxurious Vincent House health spa run by 75-year-old Frances Vincent and her children Kate and Jeff - however, disaster strikes when Laura and Rosemary are invited for supper along with Maggie, the local florist and granddaughter of Alexander Cobbe, who designed the original Dell, and Frances is found strangled in her study. In addition to those on the scene, suspicions fall on Kate's no-good husband Gary and Kate's secret lover - nursery owner Pete Farmer - before Rosemary and Laura solve the mystery when they discover a secret floral code and a past tragedy that haunts The Dell. Meanwhile, Kate herself is arrested and the murderer moves in on Laura...

programme.title: Rosemary & Thyme
programme.desc: Light-hearted drama series about a pair of gardening enthusiasts-turned-amateur sleuths. Rosemary and Laura arrive at Bowden Grange Language School to try to cure a problem with the lawn - while searching for a Polish student called Angelica, who has gone missing, Rosemary and Laura discover a body in a deserted lane. It turns out not to be that of Angelica, but of the school's handyman Felix instead - after being questioned by the police, Angelica is later found stretched out below her bedroom window irrevocably dead. The police find a knife in Angelica's suitcase and determine that she must have murdered Felix before killing herself because of the guilt - when this seems all a bit too neat for Rosemary and Laura, the pair must unravel a confusing pictogram and look into military links to piece together the clues to both murders.

programme.title: McDonald & Dodds
programme.desc: Detective drama series following two detectives who seemingly have nothing in common but are thrown together and forge a rumbustious partnership in Bath. McDonald and Dodds are sucked into the fast and furious world of Formula One after a promising young driver dies in suspicious circumstances.

programme.title: Agatha Christie's Marple
programme.desc: Crime thriller featuring the author's much-loved spinster sleuth. Gwenda Halliday has never been to England before - when she moves into the house of her dreams, she begins to have ghostly apparitions. On discovering that Gwenda grew up in the house as a child, the mystery takes a more sinister edge as she and Miss Marple realise that an unsolved murder was committed in the house.

programme.title: Midsomer Murders
programme.desc: Barnaby and Jones are reluctantly taking part in a team-building weekend run by Midsomer Yeomanry's Territorial Army, under the command of Colonel Matt Parkes, a haulage boss. The policemen are called away to investigate an explosion at Parkes' haulage yard. The yard is run by Parkes' shady son James, who has a reputation for being ruthless both in business and with women. Barnaby and Jones suspect arson. Is James capable of the crime? He has a lot of enemies, including the grieving mother of a girl who killed herself two years before. But when a man he was seen arguing with turns up dead, James falls under suspicion. More is yet to come, as the TA begin their war games - which may turn more realistic than they are supposed to be.

programme.title: Midsomer Murders
programme.desc: Murder mystery drama. The revival of a traditional festival to banish evil spirits in Midsomer Parva ends in tragedy when curate Alex Dakin is burned alive inside the straw effigy of a woman atop a bonfire - amid rumours of witchcraft, further demises caused by apparent spontaneous combustion, and a relationship frowned upon by village traditionalists, Barnaby and Scott must determine if the answer lies in a series of unjust executions in the 17th century or a more modern vendetta instead.

programme.title: Code of Silence (Signed Version)
programme.desc: Suspenseful crime drama. Alison faces a dangerous crossroads as her feelings for Liam threaten to undermine the police's investigation - after learning Liam has been using her to mislead the police about the heist by hacking her hearing aids, Alison races to the gang's true target of the Hawthorne Estate Hotel. Meanwhile, the gang led by Braden executes their plan to steal the Joshi necklace using chaos and Liam's hacking to breach security - Alison confronts Liam amidst the heist, but Braden's ruthlessness leads to the shooting of DC Ben Lawford. Alison, who is devastated by the violence, has no choice but to watch Liam escape as armed police close in - interrogated by James and Ashleigh, Alison clears her name but is left reeling when it is revealed that Liam and Braden both evaded capture with Liam double-crossing the gang and escaping with the jewels.

programme.title: Code of Silence (Signed Version)
programme.desc: Suspenseful crime drama. Alison and Julie are moved to a safe house while the police search for Liam and the gang, but Alison is kidnapped by Braden and used as leverage to demand that Liam return the necklace - Liam meets Braden and Helen in a tense warehouse standoff, but his double-crossing of the gang to avenge his foster father's death comes to light and it is revealed that he finally called the police to rescue Alison. Liam signals to Alison in BSL and enables her to escape as armed police storm the building - despite his role in exposing the gang, Liam is arrested for his involvement in the heist. Months later, Liam communicates in BSL at his trial to Alison and signals his hope in continuing the relationship with Alison even if it's in custody. Alison begins anew with her mother, receiving a financial reward from the police for her role in returning the Joshi jewels and encouragement from James to pursue work as a forensic lipreader as she looks to new horizons.

programme.title: Midsomer Murders
programme.desc: Faith Alexander arrives in Midsomer Deverell to meet her long-lost uncle Rex Masters, but Rex has told everyone that Faith's entire family was wiped out in a plane crash and he has disappeared - thanks to Miles King, an old friend who does Rex's gardening, news spreads quickly as Rex's cook Eileen Carnack, cleaner Janet Bailey, and Janet's fiance Jeremy Thacker rush to his home at Stonecroft House. Faith is anxious and can't understand how Rex has photographs of her when they haven't met following a 40-year family feud - after Barnaby asks Jones to investigate the disappearance, Rex's body is found in a river below Crofter's Weir. As Barnaby returns to Rex's study and finds some important clues in a set of risque photographs from the 1960s, can he get to the bottom of the tangled relationships stretching across the generations, learn the truth about Rex's life of mischief-making, and identify the killer?

programme.title: Vera
programme.desc: Crime drama series based on characters created by Ann Cleeves. DCI Vera Stanhope is called out to the banks of the River Tyne, where the body of a young woman has been washed up - although a popular manager at a local green energy company, a loyal friend and a loving mother and wife, Vera uncovers a web of intrigue, betrayal and lost promises as she has to unravel the victim's complicated personal and professional lives to find her killer.

programme.title: DCI Banks
programme.desc: The first part of a two-part crime thriller. What lies behind the door of number 27 The Hill? A routine 'domestic' call-out to this house leads to a policeman's murder, a grievous injury to the prime suspect for the murder, and the location of a shocking discovery. All of these events will add up to the most difficult case of DCI Alan Banks's life - five young girls have gone missing and he has just discovered the lair of a serial rapist and killer, but only four bodies are found. This means that one victim - Leanne Wray - could still be alive... however, with suspect Marcus Payne in a coma, the ambitious DS Annie Cabbot from Professional Standards wants to know why. As Banks pursues his line of investigation, she must therefore tail him every step of the way...

programme.title: DCI Banks
programme.desc: Conclusion of the two-part pilot case of the crime drama. The suspected murderer Marcus Payne dies without regaining consciousness, but his wife Lucy recovers from the injuries caused by her husband - Banks must find the missing Leanne Wray in the meantime, but time is against him and he is hindered by DS Annie Cabbot, who wants to spend time investigating the now-suspicious death of the killer. Banks knows that Payne must have had an accomplice and he questions Lucy, only for her to flirt with Banks and play him emotionally - Banks decides to take her in for questioning on the grounds of being involved with the rape and murder of the four girls and the disappearance of Leanne. However, when things gets complicated after Lucy's background and true identity is revealed, there's another question to be answered - can a victim also be guilty of a crime?

programme.title: The Taking of Pelham 123
programme.desc: Thrilling crime action remake of the classic 1974 film The Taking of Pelham One Two Three. Four armed men hijack a subway train in Manhattan one early afternoon in New York City - their leader is called Ryder, and he connects by phone with Walter Garber, a dispatcher who is watching the train's line and who has been temporarily demoted from a supervisor position while being investigated for bribery. Ryder then demands ten million dollars within one hour... or else will start shooting hostages.

programme.title: Spider-Man: Into The Spider-Verse
programme.desc: The film introduces Brooklyn teen Miles Morales, and the limitless possibilities of the Spider-Verse, where more than one can wear the mask.

programme.title: Anacondas: The Hunt For The Blood Orchid
programme.desc: Searching feral jungles for orchids which contain youth serum scientists find anacondas who've eaten them - bigger smarter more lethal than ever.

programme.title: Exorcist: The Beginning
programme.desc: Exorcist: The Beginning

programme.title: 2 Guns
programme.desc: When an attempt to take down a drug cartel goes awry, two undercover operatives are forced to go on the run together.
65 is a Punjabi drama about justice and family struggles in a village, focusing on revenge and social issues with deep emotional and intense conflicts.
A frustrated writer struggles to keep his family alive when a series of global catastrophes threatens to annihilate mankind.

programme.title: Spider-Man: Far from Home
programme.desc: Spider-Man's European vacation is cut short by elemental attacks. Forced to partner with Nick Fury, he must rise to the occasion and save the world.

programme.title: Never Back Down: No Surrender
programme.desc: Former MMA champion Case replaces an old friend and renowned fighter and fights his toughest opponent yet - but only on his own terms.

programme.title: 28 Days Later
programme.desc: After waking from a coma, a man discovers that a deadly infection has turned most humans into ferocious beings, pushing him to struggle for survival and seek refuge in a shattered world.

programme.title: Bloodshot
programme.desc: A super-soldier resurrected with advanced nanotech seeks revenge and uncovers secrets about his past in this action-packed thriller.

programme.title: Hitch
programme.desc: A dating consultant who makes a living teaching men to woo women, finds that his game doesn't work with the columnist he is smitten with.

programme.title: Bad Boys: Ride Or Die
programme.desc: Miami detectives, defying protocol, investigate their captain's murder, uncovering a dangerous conspiracy of corruption and cartel involvement, seeking justice and vindication.

programme.title: Friends With Benefits
programme.desc: The relationship between two friends gets complicated when it turns romantic.

programme.title: Resident Evil
programme.desc: A military unit fights a powerful, out-of-control supercomputer and scientists who have mutated into flesh-eating creatures.

programme.title: Godzilla
programme.desc: An enormous radioactively mutated lizard runs rampant on the island of Manhattan.

programme.title: The Mitchells Vs The Machines
programme.desc: The Mitchells, a quirky, dysfunctional family, must put aside their differences and fight a robot apocalypse during their long-awaited road trip, all while trying to save humanity.

programme.title: Spider-Man: Homecoming
programme.desc: Peter Parker grapples with his dual identity as a teenager and the burgeoning superhero, Spider-Man, balancing the extraordinary with the ordinary challenges of youth.

programme.title: Bullet Train
programme.desc: Ladybug, a skilled assassin, boards a bullet train to retrieve a briefcase, only to face off against multiple rival killers with conflicting goals.

programme.title: The Ugly Truth
programme.desc: A chauvinist puts a romantically challenged producer through a series of outrageous tests to prove his theories about relationships.

programme.title: Goosebumps 2: Haunted Halloween
programme.desc: A spooky adventure where kids face mysterious creatures unleashed from a magical book, leading to thrilling encounters and a race to save their town.

programme.title: Anaconda
programme.desc: A documentary crew travelling through the Amazon picks up a stranded man whose hobby is to capture the giant Anaconda snake.

programme.title: Venom: The Last Dance
programme.desc: Eddie Brock and Venom face alien threats and a relentless soldier, forcing them to make a life-altering choice.

programme.title: A Few Good Men
programme.desc: A young navy lawyer investigates the death of two marines. He must battle the commanding officer of the base who doesn't want the whole story told.

programme.title: Charlie's Angels
programme.desc: The most glamorous private eyes are sent into action when electronics genius Eric Knox is kidnapped.

programme.title: Lake Placid vs. Anaconda
programme.desc: A town sheriff has to find a way to kill a gigantic crocodile and anaconda before they destroy the whole town.

programme.desc: Dharma, a gangster, is jailed for killing the man who molested his sister.

programme.title: Company
programme.desc: Mallik recruits Chandu and rises against Aslam.

programme.title: Moksha

programme.title: Roop Ki Rani Choron Ka Raja
programme.desc: Orphaned after a criminal murders their parents, two children grow up facing hardships and eventually seek revenge against their parents' killer.

programme.title: Salaami
programme.desc: Army cadet Vijay uncovers corruption, sparking a fierce battle with the police, led by the vengeful officer Gautam, threatening his career and family.

programme.title: Mawaali
programme.desc: Ramesh's love story turns dark when the industrialist is killed. Accused of the crime, he must clear his name and protect the woman he loves from heartbreak and doubt.

programme.title: Satyam Shivam Sundaram
programme.desc: Rupa is shunned due to burn scars from a fire, blamed for her mother's death, and scaring off suitors.

programme.title: Nafrat Ki Aandhi
programme.desc: Ravi convinces Sonu to testify, but their bravery puts them in the crosshairs of danger. Together, they must navigate threats while standing strong for what's right.

programme.title: Chori Chori Chupke Chupke
programme.desc: Surrogate hired by childless couple falls for father of the child.

programme.title: Allah Rakha
programme.desc: Street urchin Allah Rakha discovers that his parents are alive and that the family was separated by a mafia don. He sets out for revenge.

programme.title: Trimurti
programme.desc: Kalyani fights to save her son Nandu after his arrest, asking Jagannath for mercy and his release, hoping fairness, hope, and relief will come soon.

programme.title: Salaami
programme.desc: Army cadet Vijay uncovers corruption, sparking a fierce battle with the police, led by the vengeful officer Gautam, threatening his career and family.

programme.title: Sahibaan
programme.desc: Sahibaan loves Gopi but Vijay a womanizer wants her. When she rejects him Vijay seeks to kill Gopi.

programme.title: Coolie
programme.desc: Orphaned by a flood, Iqbal becomes a railway coolie. Haunted by memories, he searches endlessly for his mother, believing each arriving train may bring her back.

programme.title: Maine Pyar Kiya
programme.desc: Prem and Suman's love faces tough challenges when family pressures and expectations come into play. Can their bond withstand the storms, or will love be overshadowed by family drama?

programme.title: Aadmi
programme.desc: After his entire family is slaughtered and he's framed, one man's search for truth uncovers a shocking conspiracy that leads him into the heart of a national-level power game.

programme.title: Loafer
programme.desc: Ravi, an unemployed man known for brawling, is wrongly accused of killing a gangster. Abandoned by his family, he must battle alone to clear his name and prove his innocence.

programme.title: Kyon Ki...Its Fate
programme.desc: Tanvi and Anand find themselves unexpectedly falling in love due to their unique circumstances, navigating the ups and downs of their relationship while discovering deep connections along the way.

programme.title: Sanam
programme.desc: Gaurav, unloved by his family, joins the army after Narendra's death. He hopes to honor his brother's memory and finally win the respect of his distant father.

programme.title: 23 March 1931 Shaheed
programme.desc: After the brutal murder of his mentor Lala Lajpat Rai by British authorities, Bhagat Singh seeks vengeance, ultimately leading to his death sentence for killing a British official.

programme.title: Gambler
programme.desc: Though Inspector Dayashanker is lazy, he unwittingly unearths a criminal conspiracy and becomes the pride of his division overnight. However, the local gangsters turn his life into a living nightmare.

programme.title: Dil Ka Kya Kasoor
programme.desc: Arun, a talented singer, gains fame for Seema's lyrics, though they've never met. When they finally meet, their encounter unravels unexpected emotions, leading to a deep connection between them.

programme.title: Entertainment
programme.desc: Starring: Akshay Kumar,Tamannaah Bhatia. Akhil, an underdog, discovers his deceased wealthy father. Attempting to claim inheritance, he finds a dog has inherited the wealth, facing comedic obstacles.

programme.title: Raksha Bandhan
programme.desc: Starring: Akshay Kumar,Bhumi Pednekar. Lala Kedarnath flips chaat and dreams. Four sisters to marry, one Sapna to forget. Love waits while duty calls-because some hearts choose responsibility over romance.

programme.title: Pushpa 2: The Rule

programme.title: Singham Again

programme.title: Rahu Ketu

programme.title: Antim: The Final Truth

programme.title: Dono

programme.title: Uunchai
